findings.title: No positive control: DECOUPLED (a strong public claim) can issue from a battery never shown able to detect real capability loss
findings.claim: The design gates instrument admissibility only against false-COUPLED (specificity). There is no sensitivity bar anywhere: nothing requires the paired MC rule to fire on any checkpoint known to have lost capability before the battery is allowed to certify 'capability held'. A battery that is deaf by construction — and this one plausibly is: ceiling-trivial items (vowel identification, 'opposite of hot') selected at a 0.90 clean floor, scored 1.0 clean — will pass the specificity gate BECAUSE it is insensitive, then return DECOUPLED__read_neq_write_BROKEN in any world, including a genuinely coupled one. Concretely: with the prereg's own paired SE (0.07–0.102) and PAIR_DROP 0.15, a true paired price equal to the only capability price this program has ever measured (dose run coupled seed, knowledge_drop 0.0909) is detected per-seed at only ~20–30%; the majority verdict in that genuinely-priced world is DECOUPLED roughly 80–90% of the time. The mirror of attempt 2's failure: attempt 2 shipped COUPLED from a rule that fires under the null; attempt 3 can ship DECOUPLED from a rule never shown to fire under the alternative.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 142–171: `_verdict` has VOIDs only for specificity_unmeasurable and battery_lacks_dose_specificity; no sensitivity branch. capability_battery_mc.py lines 39, 104–119: DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN 0.90 and ceiling-trivial item banks. coupling_confirm_v3.py line 210: eval_knowledge reads through tids/fids (the voided T/F channel), so the MC battery is the sole gating capability channel. PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md line 132: 'the paired rule tests a 0.15 drop against a ~0.07-0.10 paired SE'. RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md line 28: coupled seed knowledge_drop 0.0909 — below the new bar. Prereg lines 134–136 name the false-COUPLED residual ('indirect drift below the gate's resolution') and never the deafness residual.
findings.fix: Before freezing, pre-commit a positive control as a second admissibility bar symmetric to the specificity gate: a deliberately capability-destroyed checkpoint (e.g. train the same LoRA with the behavioral replay term removed, or a high-LR scramble, or ablation of a random high-rank subspace at matched budget) on which the paired MC rule MUST fire (delta >= PAIR_DROP), else the battery is inadmissible for any DECOUPLED verdict (VOID_COUPLING__battery_insensitive). Alternatively, demote the DECOUPLED branch to a scoped string that cannot ship a read-neq-write break claim without the sensitivity demonstration.

findings.title: PAIR_DROP 0.15 was set with attempt-2's paired deltas in hand (max 0.0417) on byte-identical dynamics and the same five seeds — the run is pre-decided toward DECOUPLED and the prereg does not say so
findings.claim: The prereg discloses applying the new paired rule to attempt 2's r* cells (deltas -0.0833, 0.0417, -0.0417, 0.0, 0.0) and frames the result as vindication of the paired contrast ('the old data never shows a paired capability price anywhere near 0.15'). But attempt 3 inherits the training loop verbatim, the same model, the same seeds {0,1,2,3,4}, the same split, and even carries a reproduction guard expecting matched reads to 0.02 — so the read-side dynamics are a known re-run. The threshold was therefore frozen at 3.6x the largest paired delta ever observed in the very dynamics about to be reproduced, and 1.65x the only observed knowledge price (0.0909). Unless the new MC channel behaves qualitatively differently from every capability readout so far, the expected verdict at freeze time is DECOUPLED, and the prereg presents the deltas as 'context only' without stating that implication. This is not post-result tuning of THIS run, but it is threshold selection informed by the same-seed data the run will replay, tilted toward the strong-break branch.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 88–91: 'Applied to attempt 2's own r* cells for context ... the five paired deltas are -0.0833, 0.0417, -0.0417, 0.0, 0.0 ... never shows a paired capability price anywhere near 0.15'. Lines 32–36: 'copies coupling_confirm.py's training loop verbatim ... same model Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct, seeds {0,1,2,3,4}'. Lines 122–123: reproduction guard, |delta| <= 0.02 vs attempt-2 receipt. RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md line 28: knowledge_drop 0.0909 on the coupled seed.
findings.fix: Add an explicit expected-outcome disclosure to the prereg: 'under reproduced attempt-2 dynamics and a channel-agnostic price, the anticipated verdict is DECOUPLED; the run is informative only if the MC channel diverges from the T/F channel.' Better: derive PAIR_DROP from a pre-committed minimum effect of interest with a stated power target (e.g. 80% per-seed power at the observed 0.09 price implies a bar near 0.09 with more items to shrink SE), or enlarge the item banks so a 0.15 bar has real power. If the bar stays at 0.15, the DECOUPLED branch must not carry the 'read_neq_write_BROKEN' headline (see the wording finding).

findings.title: The specificity gate's null pool is contaminated in exactly the coupled world: 'read intact' does not mean 'nothing removed', so a genuine dose-graded price VOIDs the battery and makes COUPLED structurally unreachable
findings.claim: The gate treats every read-intact matched-step pair as pure instrument noise ('nothing has been removed, so a paired capability drop there is instrument noise, not an attack price'). That premise is false: at read-intact checkpoints the accumulate arm is under full erasure pressure with nonzero and growing erased rank — and the dose run shows the read RECOVERING above threshold at ranks 10–24, so the pool includes the highest-dose checkpoints of the run. If the coupling hypothesis is true in its most natural form (price scales with accumulated rank, not with whether the read happens to be below 0.70 at that checkpoint), the paired rule fires hardest precisely on those high-rank read-intact pairs, the rate exceeds SPEC_MAX, and the run returns VOID_COUPLING__battery_lacks_dose_specificity. Net effect: COUPLED is reachable only in worlds where the price appears strictly and only at read crossings; DECOUPLED is reachable whenever the battery is quiet. The two terminal verdicts have asymmetric reachability, and the asymmetry points at the strong-break claim.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 60–61: 'nothing has been removed, so a paired capability drop there is instrument noise, not an attack price'. coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 90–106: specificity_gate pools ALL read-intact pairs regardless of erased_rank. RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md lines 43–47: accumulate arms recover to 0.8369 at rank 22 and 0.8037 at rank 14 — high-dose, read-intact checkpoints that enter the specificity pool.
findings.fix: Restrict the specificity pool to LOW-DOSE read-intact pairs (e.g. erased_rank <= the fixed arm's rank, or the first k checkpoints), where 'no attack price yet' is defensible — or report the gate's firing rate stratified by erased_rank and pre-commit the interpretation: monotone-in-rank firing among read-intact pairs is evidence OF coupling, not of instrument noise, and must route to a distinct outcome (e.g. COUPLED_dose_graded candidate or a named PARTIAL), not to the battery-inadmissibility VOID.

findings.title: SPEC_MAX 0.15 admits an instrument whose false-COUPLED family rate is within ~2.5x of the null probability used to condemn attempt 2
findings.claim: SPEC_MAX and PAIR_DROP being the same 0.15 is not itself incoherent (a rate vs a delta), and a barely-admissible battery is not coin-flip per seed. But the prereg justifies the gate with a sane-instrument figure ('pure noise fires on the order of 7 percent of pairs') while the frozen bar admits batteries firing at up to 15% per read-intact pair. At the admissibility boundary, P(false COUPLED majority, >=3 of 5 seeds at per-seed 0.15) is approximately 0.027 — about 9x the ~0.003 implied by the advertised 7% figure, and only ~2.5x below the 0.0675 five-of-five null probability that the VOID doc cited as 'a verdict that arrives this cheaply ... is not evidence'. The prereg never computes the worst-admissible case, so the gate's real guarantee is weaker than the text implies.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 69–73: 'pure noise fires on the order of 7 percent of pairs' as the justification for SPEC_MAX 0.15. capability_battery_mc.py lines 36–38: PAIR_DROP 0.15, SPEC_MAX 0.15. RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 72–74: 'the probability of every seed reading as coupled is 0.0675. A verdict that arrives this cheaply under a dose-free null is not evidence'.
findings.fix: Either tighten SPEC_MAX toward the advertised sane-instrument rate (e.g. 0.10), or add one sentence to the prereg's inference bounds stating the worst-admissible-case arithmetic: at spec rate = 0.15 the false-COUPLED majority probability is ~0.027, and any COUPLED verdict from a battery passing near the boundary must be reported with that number attached.

findings.title: Verdict string and pre-committed scope overclaim the DECOUPLED branch: 'read_neq_write_BROKEN' with 'capability held' rather than 'no price visible to this battery'
findings.claim: The inference-bounds section scopes the false-COUPLED residual explicitly ('does NOT prove the MC channel is causally isolated ... indirect drift below the gate's resolution remains possible and is named here') but contains no mirror sentence for the false-DECOUPLED residual — that a price below PAIR_DROP 0.15, or invisible to a 3–7 sub-task ceiling-trivial letter-MC proxy, remains possible. Meanwhile the frozen branch string is DECOUPLED__read_neq_write_BROKEN_by_accumulation_paired_1p5B and the pre-committed scope reads 'with capability held at the paired values'. 'Held' is a positive assertion about capability; what the instrument licenses is 'no paired drop >= 0.15 on the selected MC battery'. Since the verdict string is frozen code, a future RESULT doc inherits the overclaim verbatim.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 103–107: verdict string and scope 'with capability held at the paired values'; lines 133–136: battery-proxy caveat plus specificity-residual caveat, no sensitivity-residual caveat. coupling_confirm_v3.py line 168: the frozen string.
findings.fix: Amend the pre-committed scope sentence (in the prereg, before freeze) to: 'with no capability price at or above the paired bar of 0.15 visible to the selected MC battery' and add one inference-bounds sentence naming the deafness residual symmetric to the isolation residual. If the positive control from the fatal finding is adopted, the string may keep BROKEN conditional on that control passing; otherwise rename the branch (e.g. DECOUPLED__no_price_visible_to_MC_paired_1p5B).

findings.title: Capability price is gated only at the first dose-attributable crossing — the mildest dose of a transient phenomenon — biasing the per-seed call toward decoupled
findings.claim: r* is `attributable[0]`: the smallest-rank crossing. The dose run established the crossing is transient (read dips at rank 8 for one checkpoint, recovers by rank 10–14) — so the verdict samples capability at the single weakest point of the attack, where any dose-graded price would be smallest. Paired deltas at every matched step are reported but gate nothing, so a price that grows with rank after r* cannot move the verdict; combined with the sensitivity findings above this is a third independent tilt of the per-seed call toward decoupled. Inherited from the dose prereg's first-crossing definition, so arguably by design — flagged because attempt 3 upgrades the per-seed call into a potential read-neq-write break claim, which raises the cost of the bias.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py line 131: `pt = attributable[0]`. RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md lines 42–47: 'The break is transient. After r_star both accumulate arms RECOVER ... The verdict is defined at the FIRST crossing per the frozen prereg'. PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 120–121: full paired-delta dose-response is 'Reported, no bar'.
findings.fix: Pre-commit a reported (non-gating) secondary statistic with an interpretation rule: the maximum dose-attributable paired delta across ALL attributable crossings per seed, disclosed alongside the r* delta; if the max exceeds PAIR_DROP while the r* delta does not, the seed's decoupled call must be flagged as first-crossing-specific in the RESULT doc rather than presented as 'capability held'.

findings.title: Scored run never enforces the pre-registered '>= 3 survivors' selection admissibility (MIN_DISJOINT is decorative in the verdict path)
findings.claim: The prereg's frozen verdict branch 1 requires 'MC selection ok AND clean selected aggregate >= 0.80 AND each selected sub-task >= 0.70, else VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed' (PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 95-96; also line 48-49: 'require >= 3 survivors (else VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed)'). The harness does not implement the 'selection ok' conjunct anywhere on the scored path: battery_guard() in capability_battery_mc.py (lines 252-257) checks only 'if not selected: return False' plus the two floors -- a selection of 1 or 2 survivors passes. run_calibrate() (coupling_confirm_v3.py line 323) writes coupling_v3_mc_selected.json UNCONDITIONALLY, before the ok flag is acted on (only main() line 547 signals via exit code 2, which persists nothing). run_real() (lines 336-337) loads selmeta['selected_disjoint_mc'] and never reads selmeta['ok'], never re-checks len(selected) >= CBMC.MIN_DISJOINT, and never verifies selmeta['base_model'] against ctx['MODEL'].
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py lines 252-257: def battery_guard(...): if not selected: return False / return bool(aggregate(...) >= CLEAN_AGG_FLOOR and all(clean_scores[n] >= CLEAN_SUBTASK_FLOOR ...)). coupling_confirm_v3.py line 323: SELECTION_CACHE.write_text(json.dumps(sel, ...)) with no ok gate; lines 336-337: selmeta = json.loads(SELECTION_CACHE.read_text(...)); selected = selmeta['selected_disjoint_mc'] -- 'ok' never consulted; line 357: guard_battery = bool(CBMC.battery_guard(clean_mc, selected)). The --dry suite (run_dry, lines 425-530) exercises guard_verdict only with boolean inputs and never tests a 2-survivor selection, so the gap is invisible to the 15/15 dry receipt.
findings.fix: Before freeze: make the scored path enforce the prereg conjunct -- e.g. guard_battery = bool(len(selected) >= CBMC.MIN_DISJOINT and selmeta.get('ok') is True and selmeta.get('base_model') == ctx['MODEL'] and CBMC.battery_guard(clean_mc, selected)); alternatively add 'and len(selected) >= MIN_DISJOINT' inside battery_guard itself, and have run_calibrate refuse to write the receipt (or write it with an _INVALID suffix) when ok is False. Add a dry check with a 2-survivor selection expecting VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed. If triggered as-is (plausible: the 0.90 floor is effectively 15/16 per sub-task, and ORTH_LAST_MC/CONTAINS_MC/ALPHA_MC are spelling/tokenizer-hard for a 1.5B model), the run would issue a coupling verdict from a battery the prereg declares inadmissible -- an unearned verdict that forces a VOID later.

findings.title: 16-item quantization makes every nominal floor stricter than the stated number; prereg does not state the effective bars
findings.claim: On 16-item sub-tasks, accuracy takes values in multiples of 1/16, so DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN = 0.90 is effectively 15/16 = 0.9375 (at most one miss), and CLEAN_SUBTASK_FLOOR = 0.70 is effectively 12/16 = 0.75. 'Clears at 0.90' is attainable but only as 15/16 or 16/16; the selection bar is materially higher than the printed 0.90, which raises the realistic risk of fewer than 3 survivors (interacting with the previous finding) and is nowhere disclosed in the prereg or docstrings.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py lines 39, 42: DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN = 0.90, CLEAN_SUBTASK_FLOOR = 0.70 over banks of exactly 16 items (lines 48-175); PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md line 48: 'keep sub-tasks the CLEAN base clears at 0.90' with no mention that the attainable set makes this a one-miss (0.9375) bar. The selftest's own synthetic base (battery line 331) uses 0.9375 values, implicitly acknowledging the quantization without stating it.
findings.fix: One sentence in the prereg (and battery docstring): 'on 16 items the 0.90 selection floor is effectively 15/16 = 0.9375 (one miss), and the 0.70 sub-task floor is effectively 12/16 = 0.75' -- so the RESULT doc's reviewers cannot read the floors as more forgiving than they are, and so a <3-survivor calibration outcome is recognized as anticipated rather than anomalous.

findings.title: Docstring/prereg overstate the selftest: '490 checks' are not 490 gold recomputations -- only 120 are
findings.claim: Both the battery docstring ('`--selftest` recomputes EVERY gold from ground truth ... asserts exactly one option satisfies each item's predicate', lines 21-23) and the prereg ('`--selftest` recomputes every gold from ground truth (490 checks)', line 47) attach the 490 figure to gold recomputation. Verified by running the selftest and counting the receipt: 490/490 pass, but only 120 checks are ':label' gold recomputations (one per item, all 120 items); the rest are structural (count, balance, 4-distinct-options, gold-in-ABCD, exactly-one-correct) plus 26 synthetic logic checks on select/guard/paired rules. The selftest itself is NOT circular -- banks are hand-written literals and the predicates derive truth independently (question-text parse + string ops, Python str.upper, min() for ALPHA_MC, int multiplication for MUL_MC; the only same-file ground truth is the 16-entry _ANTONYM map, whose pairs I verified are standard antonyms) -- and I independently confirmed all 120 golds and the 4/4/4/4 (2/2/2/2 for MUL_MC) balance by hand. The claim is true in substance, wrong in arithmetic.
findings.evidence: Selftest run output: 'capability_battery_mc selftest: all_ok=True (490/490)'; parsing capability_battery_mc_selftest_INVALID.json: 120 checks end in ':label', 26 are non-item logic checks (select:*, guard:*, paired:*), remainder structural. capability_battery_mc.py lines 306-350 (_selftest), lines 21-23 (docstring); PREREG line 47.
findings.fix: Rephrase in both places to e.g. 'selftest: 490 checks, of which 120 recompute every gold from independent ground truth; the rest assert bank structure (counts, 4/4/4/4 balance, distinct options, exactly-one-correct) and the select/guard/paired rule logic'. Cheap now; in the RESULT doc a reviewer recounting the receipt will flag the discrepancy.

findings.title: CONTAINS_MC has a longest-option cue: the correct option is strictly the longest in 8 of 16 items
findings.claim: Because rare letters (X, Z, Q, W) live in longer words, the gold in CONTAINS_MC is strictly the longest option in 8/16 items (saxophone, conquest, sandwich, blanket, blizzard, mixture, firewood, textbook), tied-longest in ~6, and strictly non-longest in only 2 (banjo, horizon). A degraded model falling back on a superficial pick-the-longest heuristic scores roughly 8/16 plus a quarter of the ties (~0.59) instead of chance 0.25 -- the sub-task's floor under damage is inflated, compressing the dynamic range available to the paired contrast on this sub-task. The bias cannot grant a selection pass (0.59 is far below the 0.9375 effective floor) and the paired design applies it symmetrically to both arms, so it cannot flip a verdict on its own -- but it makes CONTAINS_MC the least dose-sensitive bank if it survives selection.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py lines 85-101: gold word lengths vs distractor lengths, e.g. line 88 ('saxophone' 9 vs candle/ribbon/turtle 6), line 90 ('conquest' 8 vs marble/sunset/tiger 5-6), line 94 ('blizzard' 8 vs valley/needle/rocket 6), line 101 ('textbook' 8 vs evening/hammock/pencil 6-7). No other bank shows a comparable systematic length cue (checked ORTH_FIRST/ORTH_LAST/VOWEL/CASE/ANTONYM/ALPHA item-by-item).
findings.fix: No code change required for admissibility (selection and the specificity gate both police the consequence). If editing before freeze is cheap: swap 3-4 distractors for length-matched words containing no target letter (e.g. 'saxophone' vs 'telephone/megaphone/xylophone'-class length-mates without X... 'telephone', 'chandelier', 'boulevard'). Otherwise, one line in the RESULT doc's battery description noting the cue and that pairing neutralizes it.

findings.title: VOWEL_MC item 7 carries the contestable 'Y' distractor while the effective floor allows only one miss
findings.claim: Item 7 of VOWEL_MC ('Which of the following letters is a vowel?', options Y/E/Z/B, gold E) uses Y as a distractor. Under the file's ground truth (_VOWELS = AEIOU) exactly one option is correct, so the selftest passes -- but 'Y is sometimes a vowel' is standard English pedagogy, and a model placing mass on the Y letter-token is not exhibiting a capability failure. Combined with the 15/16 effective selection floor (one-miss budget), this single contestable item can consume the entire miss budget for the sub-task at calibration, or add non-capability noise to checkpoint scores if VOWEL_MC is selected.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py line 110: ("Which of the following letters is a vowel?", ["Y", "E", "Z", "B"], "B") -- the only appearance of Y as a vowel-question distractor in the bank; line 272: _VOWELS = set("AEIOU").
findings.fix: Before freeze, replace Y with any unambiguous consonant not already in the item (e.g. "W" is also semi-ambiguous -- use "T": ["T", "E", "Z", "B"]). One-character change, removes the only defensibly-arguable gold in the seven gating banks.

findings.title: No sensitivity control: argmax-quantized, ceiling-easy MC battery has a pre-committed false-fire gate but no pre-committed detectability check, biasing the design toward an unearned DECOUPLED
findings.claim: The specificity gate bounds only the paired rule's FALSE-POSITIVE rate on read-intact pairs. Nothing in the prereg or harness ever establishes that the MC channel CAN register a genuine capability loss. Argmax over four letter groups is invariant to any perturbation that shrinks margins without flipping the winner, and the selected sub-tasks are trivially easy by construction (selection floor 0.90 on the clean base; attempt 2's analogous selection landed three sub-tasks all at 1.0). An instrument that is quantized, saturated at ceiling, and unvalidated for sensitivity fails silently in exactly one direction: paired deltas stay under PAIR_DROP and every attributable seed reads 'decoupled'. DECOUPLED is the branch that ships 'read_neq_write_BROKEN_by_accumulation' -- a headline break claim -- so the missing mirror-control is the exact structural gap that killed attempt 2, reflected: attempt 2's battery could not tell dose from noise; attempt 3's battery is never shown able to tell capability-loss from anything.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py lines 216-229: measure_mc_subtask scores by argmax only ('scores = [max(logits[i] for i in g) for g in groups]; correct += int(LETTERS[int(np.argmax(scores))] == gold)') -- no margin, no calibration of dynamic range. PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 56-73 (Fix 2) define admissibility solely as false-fire rate <= SPEC_MAX on read-intact pairs; lines 133-136 concede only 'The battery is a proxy... not an exhaustive one' and name indirect drift 'below the gate's resolution' -- the specificity side -- while no sentence anywhere pre-commits a positive control or minimum detectable effect for the break rule. coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 142-171: the DECOUPLED branch (line 167-168) issues from the same unvalidated-for-sensitivity instrument. The T/F overlay (prereg lines 52-54) shows only that the perturbation moves T/F, not that MC would move under genuine capability destruction.
findings.fix: Before freeze, add a pre-committed detectability calibration to --calibrate: score the MC battery on a deliberately capability-degraded model (e.g., LoRA with random-direction updates at norm matched to the honesty run's final adapter, or a few high-LR scramble steps) and require the selected aggregate to fall by >= PAIR_DROP, written to the frozen calibration receipt; a battery that cannot register a known lobotomy is inadmissible for the DECOUPLED branch the same way a non-specific one is inadmissible for COUPLED. Alternatively, downgrade the DECOUPLED branch string to a bounded form ('no paired MC cost >= 0.15 detected') in the frozen verdict table so the shipped claim matches what the instrument can carry.

findings.title: Specificity gate conditions on the read being intact, so shared read/MC checkpoint-level noise is excluded from the false-fire estimate but drives the COUPLED call at r*
findings.claim: The gate estimates the paired rule's noise rate ONLY on checkpoints where accumulate private-13 >= 0.70, then applies the rule at r* -- checkpoints selected precisely because private-13 < 0.70. If the LoRA perturbation magnitude fluctuates checkpoint-to-checkpoint and both readouts load on it (the residual channel-sharing the prereg itself admits is possible: 'LoRA on attention+MLP can still move letter logits indirectly'), then conditioning on a read-crossing selects high-perturbation checkpoints where the MC aggregate is also depressed for non-capability reasons. The false-fire rate measured on the low-perturbation (read-intact) stratum systematically underestimates the fire rate on the crossing stratum, so the gate can pass while COUPLED fires from shared-channel covariance rather than an attack price. This is attempt 2's failure moved one level up: from shared tokens to shared perturbation-magnitude noise, and the new rail does not see it.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 96-101: 'if not p.get("bit") or p["private13"] < SURVIVAL: continue' -- every crossing checkpoint is excluded from the specificity denominator; lines 109-139 (_seed_outcome) apply paired_broke exactly and only at a crossing checkpoint. capability_battery_mc.py lines 13-14 concede the indirect path exists. PREREG lines 133-136 name 'indirect drift below the gate's resolution' but never the selection asymmetry between the gate's population (read-intact) and the verdict's population (read-crossed). Attempt 2's own data shows read and battery moving together at constant dose (RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md matched-step table, lines 54-68: seeds 2/3/4 control read and control battery depressed together), i.e., the joint-wobble null is live, not hypothetical.
findings.fix: Pre-commit a covariance diagnostic alongside the gate, frozen now: among read-intact pairs, report the paired MC delta stratified by the accumulate read level (e.g., intact-low 0.70-0.78 vs intact-high >= 0.78) and require the low-stratum fire rate to also sit <= SPEC_MAX, or at minimum freeze it as a named reported diagnostic that the RESULT doc must disclose next to any COUPLED call, so a delta-vs-read gradient among intact points is on the record before the verdict is read. This costs no GPU time -- it is a re-cut of pairs the gate already computes.

findings.title: Content-label token collision in VOWEL_MC / CASE_MC / ALPHA_MC: option contents are themselves A-D-adjacent letter tokens, so the readout cannot distinguish label-answering from content-answering
findings.claim: In three of seven gating sub-tasks the option STRINGS are single letters, sometimes literally A, B, C, or D. The scorer reads argmax over the label tokens A/B/C/D, so a model that answers with the content letter instead of the label letter is scored by accident. CASE_MC is worst: the correct CONTENT is always a letter, and answering it directly is the most natural completion ('uppercase form of b?' -> 'B'), yet for 12 of 16 items the content letter differs from its label (e.g., content B at label A is scored as wrong via the label-B group). The honesty LoRA trains one-word-answer formatting on a near-identical instruction template ('Answer with exactly one word: True or False' vs 'Answer with exactly one letter: A, B, C, or D'), so fine-tuning plausibly shifts label-vs-content answering style. The paired contrast subtracts arm-common shifts and the specificity gate bounds resulting noise, so this cannot silently flip a verdict -- but it injects fine-tune-movable measurement variance into exactly the channel the design claims is isolated, spending specificity-gate headroom on an avoidable confound.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py line 122: ("Which of the following is the uppercase form of the letter 'b'?", ["B", "D", "P", "R"], "A") -- the correct content string 'B' sits at label A while token 'B' is the label group for the WRONG option 'D'. Line 104: VOWEL_MC options ["A", "B", "D", "F"] are literal label-alphabet tokens. Lines 157-174: ALPHA_MC options are all single letters, many in {A,B,C,D}. letter_token_ids (lines 203-213) builds groups from bare/space-prefixed 'A'..'D' with no disambiguation from option content. mc_prompt (lines 195-200) renders both channels into the same next-token position.
findings.fix: Before freeze, replace single-letter option contents with multi-character renderings that tokenize away from the label alphabet (e.g., quote the content: A) 'B'  B) 'D' -- or spell 'the letter B'), or restrict letter-content items to option letters outside {A,B,C,D}. Re-run --selftest; no other change needed.

findings.title: The 'no direct gradient / shares no tokens' isolation claim is mechanistically overstated: the honesty loss's log_softmax partition function gives every letter logit a direct gradient, and letter-group/TF-group disjointness is asserted but never asserted at runtime
findings.claim: The behavioral term is -logsumexp(logp[T-or-F ids]) with logp = log_softmax over the full vocabulary; the normalizer term's gradient with respect to EVERY logit -- including A/B/C/D -- is softmax(z)_i > 0, so letter logits are pushed down by the training objective at every training step (only the frozen unembedding ROWS receive no gradient, because lm_head is outside the LoRA target modules). The battery's isolation premise therefore rests on (a) argmax-over-letters being invariant to near-uniform suppression and (b) the empirical gate -- not on the absence of gradient the docs assert. Separately, the code never checks that the four letter groups are disjoint from tids/fids (or from each other): 'shares no tokens with the training target' is a tokenizer-dependent claim about Qwen2.5's BPE that is currently ungrounded by any receipt, and the RESULT doc will quote it.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py lines 10-11: 'The letter tokens receive no direct gradient from the honesty objective.' PREREG lines 43-44: 'the channel shares no tokens with the training target.' coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 256-259: 'logp = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1); losses = [-torch.logsumexp(logp[i, ...' -- the softmax normalizer couples all vocab logits into the loss. letter_token_ids (capability_battery_mc.py lines 203-213) and run_real (coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 287-288, 299) build letter groups and tids/fids with no disjointness assertion between them.
findings.fix: Two-line fix in _load_base_and_clean or letter_token_ids: assert set().union(*groups).isdisjoint(set(tids) | set(fids)) and assert pairwise-disjoint groups, and record the resolved token ids in the calibration receipt. Reword the docstring/prereg sentence to what is true and sufficient: 'no trainable unembedding parameters and no selective per-letter gradient; the objective suppresses all non-T/F logits uniformly through the softmax normalizer, which argmax-within-letters is invariant to; residual differential movement is what the specificity gate measures.'

findings.title: No per-pair 'points' receipt list for the specificity rate; prereg calls the gate's record a 'ledger' and the cache is named _cells.jsonl -- certifier-vocabulary friction for the future RESULT doc
findings.claim: The specificity block in the result JSON stores only aggregate counts (n_pairs, n_fired, rate, mean/sd); the per-pair records (seed, step, acc read, fixed read, delta, fired) are never written anywhere, so the verdict-gating rate is re-derivable only by re-running gate logic over curves. Under the house certifier rule that per-cell receipt lists grounding numbers must be named 'points' (and that 'cells'/'rows'/'ledger'-named lists cannot ground), the RESULT doc's central number (fired/pairs on a trigger-vocab line) has no conforming receipt list to point at. The prereg additionally pre-commits the word 'ledger' for exactly this record, steering the future RESULT toward banned vocabulary.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 103-106: specificity_gate returns only {'n_pairs', 'n_fired', 'rate', 'max_allowed', 'min_pairs', 'mean_delta', 'sd_delta'} -- the deltas list (line 101) is aggregated and discarded, no per-pair list. PREREG line 124: 'The specificity gate's full ledger: n_pairs, n_fired, rate, mean/sd of read-intact deltas.' Line 64 of coupling_confirm_v3.py: CELLS_CACHE = HERE / 'coupling_confirm_v3_cells.jsonl'.
findings.fix: Before freeze, add a 'points' key to the specificity dict: a list of per-pair records {seed, step, acc_read, fixed_read, delta, fired}; change the prereg word 'ledger' to 'points list'. Leave the cache filename if the certifier only inspects the result JSON, but renaming to _points.jsonl is free insurance.

findings.title: 'Verbatim copy ... the SOLE new delta' claim is inaccurate: v3's audit() also removes attempt 2's T/F aggregate lines, and the console tf_agg is computed over a different sub-task set than attempt 2's frozen selection
findings.claim: The single-variable discipline claim says the training loop is a verbatim copy with one added line. The gradient loop is indeed byte-equivalent and the added MC measurement consumes no RNG (argmax under no_grad), so reproducibility of the seed-0/1 reproduction guard is safe. But audit() has two deltas, not one: the MC call is ADDED and attempt 2's bat['aggregate'] / bat['aggregate_adjacent'] lines are REMOVED, so the stored T/F battery dicts in v3 receipts lack the aggregate keys attempt 2's receipts carry -- a shape change in the mechanism receipt the prereg promises to overlay against the old channel. Separately, the progress print aggregates the T/F battery over whatever DISJOINT_POOL keys are present rather than any frozen selection (print-only, gates nothing).
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py line 179 docstring: 'Verbatim copy of coupling_confirm.train_accumulating ...; the SOLE new delta is the CBMC.measure_all_mc call inside audit().' coupling_confirm.py lines 154-155: 'bat["aggregate"] = CB.aggregate(bat, selected); bat["aggregate_adjacent"] = ...' -- absent from v3 audit() (lines 205-221). v3 line 402: tf_agg computed as CB.aggregate(tail['battery'], [k for k in tail['battery'] if k in CB.DISJOINT_POOL]).
findings.fix: Either restore the two T/F aggregate lines in v3's audit() (making the copy claim true and the channel-vs-channel overlay directly comparable to attempt 2's receipts), or amend the docstring and prereg line 32-33 to name both deltas: one addition, one removal. Restoring is preferable -- the overlay is the run's mechanism receipt and should not require recomputing aggregates from per-sub-task values with off-receipt code.

findings.title: Prereg's 'MC selection ok' (>= 3 survivors) guard conjunct is unenforceable in the scored run
findings.claim: Prereg guard 1 freezes 'MC selection ok AND clean selected aggregate >= 0.80 AND each selected sub-task >= 0.70, else VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed', and Fix 1 defines selection-ok as 'require >= 3 survivors (else VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed)'. The harness never checks the >=3-survivor condition on the scored path: a frozen selection with 1-2 survivors would pass the battery guard and the run would score on an inadmissible battery, and the VOID branch frozen for exactly that contingency can never fire for it.
findings.evidence: C:\Users\heyzo\clawd\styxx\papers\calib-poison-general\capability_battery_mc.py lines 252-257: battery_guard only checks `if not selected: return False` plus the 0.80/0.70 floors -- no MIN_DISJOINT. C:\Users\heyzo\clawd\styxx\papers\calib-poison-general\coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 336-337: run_real loads `selmeta["selected_disjoint_mc"]` and never reads `selmeta["ok"]` or compares len(selected) to CBMC.MIN_DISJOINT. Line 323: run_calibrate writes coupling_v3_mc_selected.json unconditionally, even when ok=False (only the process exit code 2 signals failure, and run_real gates on file existence alone, line 332). Prereg: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 48-49 and 94-96. Attempt 2 (coupling_confirm.py / capability_battery.py lines 159-166) has the same inherited gap, but prereg3 re-freezes the >=3 requirement explicitly. --dry never exercises battery_guard computation (only boolean guard_verdict), so the gap is invisible to the 15 checks.
findings.fix: Before freezing: make battery_guard (or run_real's guard_battery computation) also require `len(selected) >= CBMC.MIN_DISJOINT` (equivalently, refuse a selection receipt with ok=false: `guard_battery = selmeta["ok"] and CBMC.battery_guard(...)`), and add a dry/selftest case where a 2-survivor selection yields VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed.

findings.title: Prereg-committed channel-vs-channel overlay has no stored T/F aggregate and no frozen T/F aggregation
findings.claim: The prereg commits to reporting 'the channel-vs-channel overlay: T/F battery aggregate vs MC aggregate, both arms, every seed (the mechanism receipt)', but v3 deleted the checkpoint-level T/F aggregate that attempt 2 stored and loads no frozen T/F selection, so the overlay's T/F aggregation composition is left to post-hoc discretion at RESULT time.
findings.evidence: Diff of train_accumulating: coupling_confirm.py lines 154-155 stored `bat["aggregate"] = CB.aggregate(bat, selected)` (over the frozen T/F selection) and `bat["aggregate_adjacent"]`; coupling_confirm_v3.py audit() (lines 211-213) drops both (necessarily -- `selected` now holds MC names). The only T/F aggregate v3 computes is a console print, coupling_confirm_v3.py line 402, using `[k for k in tail['battery'] if k in CB.DISJOINT_POOL]` -- the full pool, not attempt 2's frozen selection in coupling_confirm_disjoint_selected.json. Prereg commitment: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 118-119. Per-subtask T/F scores ARE stored (audit line 218), so the overlay is derivable; only the aggregation choice is unfrozen. Gates nothing (report-only), so no verdict impact.
findings.fix: Either store the T/F aggregate at each checkpoint computed over attempt 2's frozen selection (load coupling_confirm_disjoint_selected.json), or add one sentence to the prereg naming which T/F aggregation the overlay will use (e.g. 'attempt 2's frozen T/F selection'), so the mechanism receipt is not assembled with post-hoc freedom.

findings.title: 'Verbatim copy ... SOLE new delta is one added audit line' is inaccurate
findings.claim: Both the harness docstring and the prereg claim the training loop is copied verbatim with ONE added audit line; the actual diff shows two lines added (MC measure + MC aggregate) and two lines removed (T/F aggregate + aggregate_adjacent), plus one new return-dict key. The gradient/attack code path is byte-identical, so the science claim holds, but the RESULT doc will repeat the 'verbatim / one line' wording and it is falsifiable against the diff.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 25-26 ('copied verbatim from coupling_confirm.train_accumulating with ONE added audit line') and 179-180 ('Verbatim copy ... the SOLE new delta is the CBMC.measure_all_mc call'); PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md line 32 ('copies coupling_confirm.py's training loop verbatim'). Diff of coupling_confirm.py lines 117-206 vs coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 177-266: only divergences are inside audit() -- v2's `bat["aggregate"]`/`bat["aggregate_adjacent"]` lines removed, `mc = CBMC.measure_all_mc(...)` and `mc["aggregate"] = ...` added, `battery_mc` key added to the return dict, plus the added `letter_groups` parameter. Everything from `checkpoints = [audit(0)]` through `opt.step()` is byte-identical.
findings.fix: Reword both docstrings and the prereg sentence to: 'training/attack path copied verbatim; audit() adds the MC measurement (two lines + one return key) and drops the now-inapplicable T/F aggregate lines' -- so the RESULT doc's provenance claim matches the diff exactly.

findings.title: VOID_COUPLING__no_calibration is a returnable verdict string absent from the frozen verdict set
findings.claim: run_real can return verdict 'VOID_COUPLING__no_calibration', which appears nowhere in the prereg's frozen verdict list and is not exercised by --dry. It fires only when the operator skips --calibrate, and it blocks rather than grants (safe direction), but it is an out-of-prereg verdict string in the result JSON namespace.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 332-334: `return {"verdict": "VOID_COUPLING__no_calibration", ...}`. PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md 'Frozen verdict' section (lines 92-112) enumerates the verdict set; no_calibration is not in it (inherited from coupling_confirm.py lines 269-271, where it was likewise unpreregistered).
findings.fix: Add one line to the prereg's artifacts/verdict section acknowledging the operational precondition verdict ('scored run without a committed calibration receipt returns VOID_COUPLING__no_calibration and is not a scored outcome'), or rename it to a non-verdict error field so the verdict namespace stays exactly the frozen set.

findings.title: Specificity gate is measured on a population systematically unlike the verdict point; attempt-2's own data shows the fire rate covaries with read status, so the gate can certify an unearned COUPLED
findings.claim: The gate estimates the paired rule's false-fire rate only on pairs where the accumulate read is INTACT (private-13 >= 0.70 AND bit) and puts no condition on the control read. The verdict point is conditioned the opposite way on both arms: accumulate read CROSSED (< 0.70) AND control read intact (>= 0.70). If the battery co-fluctuates with the read (shared checkpoint-level drift), the fire rate at r*-like points exceeds the gate-measured rate, and the double selection at r* (accumulate read on a downward fluctuation, control read on an upward one) plus regression to the mean biases the paired delta fixed-minus-accumulate upward -- i.e. toward COUPLED -- in a way the gate structurally cannot see. Attempt 2's receipts quantify exactly this covariation: the break rule fired on 8 of 8 read-BROKEN accumulate checkpoints (45-37 fired out of 60-52 checkpoints) versus 37/52 = 0.7115 read-intact, and on the control arm 4/6 read-broken vs 31/54 = 0.5741 intact. A gate reading, say, 0.10 on intact pairs is compatible with near-certain firing at crossing points, so the run can pass its own admissibility rail and return a COUPLED that is still instrument-driven -- the exact failure mode attempt 3 was built to prevent.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 58-62 ('where the accumulate arm's read is INTACT (bit=True and private-13 >= SURVIVAL 0.70 -- nothing has been removed...)'); coupling_confirm_v3.py line 98 ('if not p.get("bit") or p["private13"] < SURVIVAL: continue') vs lines 118-123 (r* requires acc private13 < SURVIVAL AND fixed private13 >= SURVIVAL); RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 31-48: accumulate 45/60 fired overall but 37/52 on read-intact => 8/8 on read-broken; control 35/60 overall, 31/54 intact => 4/6 broken.
findings.fix: Before freezing: (a) condition the gate pairs symmetrically with the verdict (also require control read >= 0.70 in the gate population, matching the r* pair on that arm); (b) add a pre-committed dose/read-stratified diagnostic to admissibility -- e.g. the fire rate must not be significantly higher in the lowest-read-intact tercile than the highest, or report Spearman(read, paired delta) with a pre-committed bar -- so read-correlated firing voids the battery even when the pooled intact-rate passes; (c) at minimum, name this conditioning mismatch in the inference-bounds section instead of only 'indirect drift below the gate's resolution'.

findings.title: Held-at-r* is scored as an affirmative DECOUPLED verdict from a ~1.5-sigma test; the 5-seed majority amplifies rather than fixes the asymmetry, so a real capability price of 0.10 returns the BREAK verdict ~82% of the time
findings.claim: The per-seed rule is a one-sided 1.47-sigma test (PAIR_DROP 0.15 against paired SE 0.102 at minimum selection): power is 0.50 when the true paired drop equals the bar exactly, 0.31 at a true drop of 0.10, 0.16 at 0.05. Failure to fire is mapped directly to 'decoupled' -- a positive claim ('capability held') with no lower confidence bound. Because all five seeds share the same true effect, majority voting concentrates on the more-probable side of the per-seed test instead of adding power: with a true drop of 0.10 across seeds, P(DECOUPLED majority) ~= 0.82 (per-seed coupled 0.312, binomial over 5); at a true drop exactly at the bar, the run flips a coin between COUPLED and DECOUPLED__read_neq_write_BROKEN. The prereg's own justification -- 'a single-seed call is ~1.5-2 sigma, which is why only the 5-seed majority speaks' -- is statistically backwards for this failure mode: the majority protects against independent per-seed noise, not against a shared sub-bar effect. Under pure noise (fire rate 0.0707/seed) with five admissible crossing seeds, P(DECOUPLED majority) = 0.997 -- the design's default output in a null world is its most sensational claim, a scope-limited read!=write BREAK.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 84-87 ('Broke -> coupled; held -> decoupled'), lines 102-109 (strict majority, DECOUPLED = 'A BREAK'), lines 130-133 ('paired rule tests a 0.15 drop against a ~0.07-0.10 paired SE -- a single-seed call is ~1.5-2 sigma, which is why only the 5-seed majority speaks'). Computed: per-seed detection Phi-based {drop 0.05: 0.164, 0.10: 0.312, 0.15: 0.500}; P(DECOUPLED majority | true drop 0.10, 5 crossing seeds) = 0.821; P(DECOUPLED majority | pure noise) = 0.997.
findings.fix: Before freezing, make DECOUPLED an earned verdict rather than the complement of COUPLED: per seed call 'decoupled' only if the paired delta's upper confidence bound clears the bar (e.g. delta + 1.5*SE_paired < PAIR_DROP, an equivalence-style criterion), with the middle band scored 'undetermined' (counts toward PARTIAL). Alternatively keep the binary rule but demote the DECOUPLED branch string from 'read_neq_write_BROKEN' to a bounded 'no paired price detected at resolution 0.15', matching what the test can actually support.

findings.title: The gate's premise 'read intact = nothing has been removed' is false: post-crossing recovered checkpoints are high-dose read-intact pairs, so a genuinely coupled world with persistent capability damage fails the specificity gate and gets mislabeled as instrument failure
findings.claim: Erasure dose (accumulated rank) grows monotonically from step 25 regardless of whether the read is above 0.70, and the dose run showed the read RECOVERS above the survival bar after r* (seed 0: 0.7346 at rank 10, 0.8369 at rank 22; seed 1: 0.7465 at rank 10, 0.8037 at rank 14). Those late checkpoints -- rank 10-24, read intact -- enter the specificity gate's denominator. If coupling is real and capability damage persists (or grows) with dose while the read transiently recovers -- the most natural coupled world -- those pairs fire the paired rule, the pooled rate exceeds SPEC_MAX 0.15, and the run returns VOID_COUPLING__battery_lacks_dose_specificity with the frozen interpretation 'the MC channel is ALSO noise-dominated'. That claim about the instrument would be wrong: the firing would be dose-response, not noise. Structurally, COUPLED is reachable only if capability damage is transient and co-located with the read's crossing; the design's strongest-coupling worlds are routed into an instrument-failure VOID whose stated cause is incorrect.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 59-61 ('the accumulate arm's read is INTACT ... nothing has been removed, so a paired capability drop there is instrument noise, not an attack price') and lines 64-66 (VOID interpretation 'the MC channel is ALSO noise-dominated'); RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md lines 42-45 ('After r_star both accumulate arms RECOVER: seed 0 climbs back to 0.7346 at rank 10 and reaches 0.8369 at rank 22 ... seed 1 climbs to 0.7465 at rank 10'); coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 97-99 (gate includes every bit, read-intact accumulate checkpoint at any rank).
findings.fix: Before freezing, restrict the gate population to pre-crossing / low-dose read-intact pairs (e.g. checkpoints before the seed's first crossing, or erased_rank <= the rank at first crossing minus one refit), or stratify the gate rate by rank and pre-commit both branches' interpretations: flat-in-rank firing -> instrument noise (VOID as written); rank-increasing firing among read-intact pairs -> reportable dose-response evidence, not a battery indictment. Rewrite the 'nothing has been removed' rationale, which is factually wrong for recovered checkpoints.

findings.title: Dose-attributability of r* is a single unbuffered comparison of two SE-0.06 reads scanned over ~12 checkpoints, with no multiplicity control -- noise manufactures admissible crossings
findings.claim: r* is the first checkpoint where accumulate read < 0.70 AND control read >= 0.70, evaluated point-by-point over roughly 12-13 post-bite checkpoints per seed. Each read carries SE ~0.06 (EVAL n=66), and every crossing in the program's history sits within ~1 SE of the bar (0.671, 0.6756 vs 0.70; control non-attributable values 0.6811, 0.6571, 0.635). When both arms' true reads hover near 0.70, the AND-condition is satisfied by two independent ~1-SE fluctuations at some checkpoint with high probability across the scan, so a noise excursion gets promoted to a dose-attributable r* and the whole verdict is then scored at a regression-to-the-mean point. The prereg discloses thin margins ('crossings within ~1 SE of the bar remain thin-margin and are reported as such') but the decision rule takes no margin and applies no scan correction -- reporting does not gate. Combined with the DECOUPLED-by-default rule this makes the BREAK verdict the modal outcome of a no-dose-effect world.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 78-81 (r* definition, pointwise), line 129 ('per-checkpoint AUROC SE ~ 0.06 -- crossings within ~1 SE of the bar remain thin-margin and are reported as such'); coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 118-123 (bare comparisons p['private13'] < SURVIVAL and fixed >= SURVIVAL, no margin); RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 54-60 (all five r* reads 0.6295-0.6756 and three control reads 0.635-0.6811, everything within ~1 SE of 0.70).
findings.fix: Before freezing, require attributability with margin or persistence: e.g. control read minus accumulate read >= 1 SE (0.06) at the matched step, or the accumulate crossing must hold on two consecutive checkpoints, or the control must be intact at the crossing step AND its neighbors. Pre-commit whichever is chosen; disclose in the prereg that the pointwise rule was rejected for its scan multiplicity.

findings.title: The VOID_crossing_not_dose_attributable exclusion is direction-correlated: in attempt 2's observed world it removes exactly the three decoupled-direction seeds and shrinks the majority denominator
findings.claim: Walking attempt 2's world through the attempt-3 rule: seeds 2, 3, 4 crossed at steps where the control read was also below 0.70 (0.6811, 0.6571, 0.635), so their crossings are non-attributable; absent a later attributable crossing each becomes VOID_crossing_not_dose_attributable and leaves the denominator. Those three seeds' paired MC-context deltas are -0.0417, 0.0, 0.0 -- all on the decoupled side of the bar -- which is not a coincidence: a step where the control read crossed is a step of heavy shared drift where the control battery is also depressed, so the paired delta (control minus accumulate) is small. The exclusion therefore systematically removes decoupled-leaning seeds AND lowers the majority bar: with 2 exclusions, COUPLED can issue from 2 of 5 seeds (n_adm=3, 2 > 1.5). In the exact 3-exclusion attempt-2 world the run lands n_adm=2 -> VOID_COUPLING__underpowered -- honest, but it means the design likely cannot return any coupling verdict in the one world the program has already observed, and the boundary between 'no verdict' and 'COUPLED from a 2-seed rump' is one seed's noisy control read.
findings.evidence: RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 54-64 (matched-step table: seeds 2/3/4 control reads 0.6811/0.6571/0.635; control battery 0.7917/0.6667/0.9167 vs accumulate 0.8333/0.6667/0.9167 => deltas -0.0417/0.0/0.0); PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 82-83 (excluded from the majority) and line 102 (strict majority over admissible seeds); coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 148-149, 158-160 (admissible excludes non-attributable; n_adm < 3 -> underpowered).
findings.fix: Before freezing: (a) pre-commit a sensitivity disclosure with teeth -- the excluded seeds' paired deltas at their (non-attributable) crossings must be reported next to the verdict, and if their direction-majority contradicts the issued majority the verdict demotes to PARTIAL; or (b) require n_admissible >= 4 for a COUPLED/DECOUPLED majority when any seed was excluded for non-attributability. Either keeps the exclusion from both silencing one direction and shrinking the bar it must clear.

findings.title: Specificity gate is weakly powered at its own floor: a noise-dominated instrument with true fire rate 0.25 passes 22.5% of the time at MIN_SPEC_PAIRS=20, and pooling ignores serial correlation and unequal per-seed pair counts
findings.claim: The gate is a point-estimate threshold (rate <= 0.15) with no confidence bound. At the frozen minimum of 20 pairs it admits fired <= 3; binomially, an instrument with true false-fire rate 0.25 passes with probability 0.225, rate 0.20 passes with 0.411, and even 0.30 passes with 0.107. The floor of 20 binds exactly in heavy-crossing worlds (few read-intact pairs), i.e. precisely when the verdict-carrying seeds are most exposed. Two further effects erode even these numbers: (a) pairs within a seed are consecutive checkpoints of one training run, so they are serially correlated and the effective sample is smaller than the count; (b) pooling across seeds lets a stable surviving seed's many intact pairs dilute the pooled rate below SPEC_MAX while the crossing seeds' own fire rates exceed it -- the verdict then rests on per-seed instruments the pooled gate never individually admitted. At realistic pair counts (~50, as in attempt 2's 52 intact checkpoints) the binomial power is adequate (true 0.25 passes only 3.3%), so the defect is concentrated at and near the frozen floor.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py lines 37-38 (SPEC_MAX = 0.15, MIN_SPEC_PAIRS = 20); coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 90-106 (pooled across all seeds, raw rate, no interval) and lines 161-164 (gate branches). Computed: P(X<=3 | n=20, p=0.25) = 0.2252; P(X<=3 | n=20, p=0.20) = 0.4114; P(X<=3 | n=20, p=0.30) = 0.1071; P(X<=7 | n=52, p=0.25) = 0.0332.
findings.fix: Before freezing, either raise MIN_SPEC_PAIRS to ~40-50 (attempt-2 geometry supplies ~52-65 pairs, so this costs nothing in the expected world), or replace the point-estimate rule with an upper-confidence-bound rule (e.g. Wilson 90% upper bound of the fire rate <= SPEC_MAX), and add a per-seed disclosure of fire rates so a pooled pass cannot mask a hot verdict-carrying seed.

findings.title: The 'pure noise fires on the order of 7 percent' margin claim counts only item-level binomial noise; attempt 2's own checkpoint dispersion exceeds the binomial ceiling, so 7% is a floor, not an estimate
findings.claim: The prereg derives 7% from the worst-case binomial SE at 48 items (0.0722 per checkpoint, 0.102 paired; 0.15/0.102 = 1.47 sigma -> 7.1% one-sided). But attempt 2's receipts show per-checkpoint battery SDs of 0.1399 (accumulate) and 0.1111 (control) -- above the binomial maximum of 0.0722 -- meaning checkpoint-level drift, not item sampling, dominates the dispersion, and pairing two independent training runs does not cancel run-specific drift. If MC drift resembles T/F drift the true noise fire rate is several times 7% and the gate fails; the prereg's 'the bar is passable with margin by a sane instrument' therefore rests on an unmeasured quantity (MC checkpoint drift), while presenting an item-noise-only calculation as the design margin.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 69-73 ('per-checkpoint aggregate SE is at most 0.072, the paired-difference SE at most 0.102, so PAIR_DROP = 0.15 sits at roughly 1.5 paired SEs and pure noise fires on the order of 7 percent of pairs'); RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 75-78 (accumulate mean 0.8035 sd 0.1399; control mean 0.8625 sd 0.1111 -- both SDs above the 48-item binomial ceiling 0.0722).
findings.fix: Reword the margin paragraph: state that 7% is the item-noise floor, that attempt 2's T/F channel showed checkpoint-level dispersion 1.5-2x the binomial ceiling, and that the gate exists precisely because the MC channel's checkpoint drift is unknown before the run. No threshold change required.

findings.title: Score quantization makes the operative PAIR_DROP threshold depend on selection size: at the minimum 3-subtask selection the effective bar is 0.1667, not 0.15
findings.claim: With k selected sub-tasks of 16 items, achievable aggregates are multiples of 1/(16k), so paired deltas are too. At k=3 the grid is 1/48: no achievable delta lies in (0.1458, 0.1667), so 'delta >= 0.15' is operationally 'delta >= 0.1667' -- 1.63 paired SEs, noise fire 5.1% rather than the stated 7.1%. At k=4 the effective bar is 0.15625 (grid 1/64); only at k=5 is 0.15 exactly achievable (12/80). The same shift applies to the specificity gate's expected fire rate. The threshold is frozen on paper but its operative severity varies with the data-dependent number of calibration survivors -- worth pre-declaring so a post-run reader cannot read the k-dependence as tuning. (The 4-decimal rounding itself is harmless: all k/16 sub-task scores are exact at 4 decimals, and no achievable delta sits within rounding distance of 0.15 at k=3 or 4.)
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py line 36 (PAIR_DROP = 0.15), lines 260-268 (paired_broke: delta = round(agg_fixed - agg_acc, 4); >= PAIR_DROP); PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 69-71 (minimum selection 3 x 16 = 48 items). Computed: 7/48 = 0.1458, 8/48 = 0.1667; z = 0.1667/0.1021 = 1.63 -> 5.1% noise fire; 4 survivors: 10/64 = 0.15625.
findings.fix: Add one sentence to the prereg's Fix 2/Fix 3 sections stating the achievable-delta grid per selection size (1/48, 1/64, 1/80) and the resulting effective bars (0.1667 / 0.15625 / 0.15), so the k-dependence of the operative threshold is frozen and disclosed rather than discovered post-result.

findings.title: Specificity VOIDs precede and can veto SURVIVES, a verdict the battery never gates
findings.claim: The frozen order places both specificity VOIDs (rate > SPEC_MAX; n_pairs < 20) before the all-survive branch. SURVIVES is determined entirely by the read never crossing -- the MC battery plays no role in it -- yet a noisy battery (or, per the ordering, an unmeasurable one) converts an all-survive world into VOID_COUPLING__battery_lacks_dose_specificity or VOID_COUPLING__specificity_unmeasurable, withholding a read-only conclusion because an instrument it does not use failed admissibility. Since an all-survive world maximizes read-intact pairs, the unmeasurable branch is unreachable there, but the noise branch is live. This costs an honest verdict rather than manufacturing one, so it is an inference/efficiency defect, not a validity defect -- but the ordering is frozen, so it must be fixed now or never.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 100-101 (step 4 'The specificity gate ... both its VOIDs precede any coupling outcome' before step 5 SURVIVES); coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 161-166 (spec checks at lines 161-164 return before the all-survive check at line 165).
findings.fix: Before freezing, either move the SURVIVES branch ahead of the specificity gate (with a note that SURVIVES carries no capability claim), or keep the order and add a sentence to the prereg acknowledging that a battery-noise VOID deliberately suppresses even battery-independent verdicts, so the choice is visibly intentional.

findings.title: No sensitivity control anywhere in the design: DECOUPLED — the headline read≠write break claim — is the default output of an instrument never shown able to detect capability loss and a ~1.5-sigma rule with no equivalence bound
findings.claim: The design gates instrument admissibility only against false-COUPLED (specificity); nothing anywhere requires the MC channel to demonstrate it CAN register genuine capability loss, and failure-to-fire is mapped directly to a positive claim ('capability held'). The defect operates at three reinforcing levels. (a) Instrument: argmax over four letter groups is invariant to any perturbation that shrinks margins without flipping the winner; the sub-tasks are ceiling-trivial by construction (selection floor 0.90 on the clean base; attempt 2's analogous selection landed all sub-tasks at 1.0); a battery that is deaf by construction passes the specificity gate BECAUSE it is insensitive, and eval_knowledge reads through the voided T/F channel, so the MC battery is the sole gating capability channel. (b) Statistics: the per-seed rule is a one-sided 1.47-sigma test (PAIR_DROP 0.15 against paired SE 0.102 at minimum selection): power is 0.50 at a true drop exactly at the bar, 0.31 at 0.10, 0.16 at 0.05 — the only capability price this program has ever measured (dose-run coupled seed knowledge_drop 0.0909) is below the bar and detected per-seed at only ~20-30%. (c) Aggregation: because all five seeds share the same true effect, majority voting concentrates on the more-probable side of the per-seed test instead of adding power: with a true drop of 0.10, P(DECOUPLED majority) ≈ 0.82; under pure noise (fire rate 0.0707/seed) P(DECOUPLED majority) = 0.997. The prereg's own justification — 'a single-seed call is ~1.5-2 sigma, which is why only the 5-seed majority speaks' — is statistically backwards for this failure mode: the majority protects against independent per-seed noise, not a shared sub-bar effect. The design's default output in a null world AND in a genuinely-priced-below-bar world is its most sensational claim, DECOUPLED__read_neq_write_BROKEN — the exact mirror of attempt 2's failure: attempt 2 shipped COUPLED from a rule that fires under the null; attempt 3 can ship DECOUPLED from a rule never shown to fire under the alternative.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 142-171: _verdict has VOIDs only for specificity_unmeasurable and battery_lacks_dose_specificity — no sensitivity branch; the DECOUPLED branch (lines 167-168) issues from the unvalidated-for-sensitivity instrument. capability_battery_mc.py lines 216-229: measure_mc_subtask scores by argmax only ('scores = [max(logits[i] for i in g) for g in groups]; correct += int(LETTERS[int(np.argmax(scores))] == gold)') — no margin, no dynamic-range calibration; lines 39, 104-119: DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN 0.90 and ceiling-trivial item banks (vowel identification, 'opposite of hot'). coupling_confirm_v3.py line 210: eval_knowledge reads through tids/fids (the voided T/F channel). PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 84-87 ('Broke -> coupled; held -> decoupled'), lines 102-109 (strict majority, DECOUPLED = 'A BREAK'), lines 130-133 ('the paired rule tests a 0.15 drop against a ~0.07-0.10 paired SE — a single-seed call is ~1.5-2 sigma, which is why only the 5-seed majority speaks'), lines 133-136 (only the false-COUPLED residual is named; the deafness residual never is). RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md line 28: coupled seed knowledge_drop 0.0909 — below the new bar. Computed: per-seed detection {drop 0.05: 0.164, 0.10: 0.312, 0.15: 0.500}; P(DECOUPLED majority | true drop 0.10, 5 crossing seeds) = 0.821; P(DECOUPLED majority | pure noise) = 0.997.
findings.fix: Before freezing, three complementary repairs, adopt at least the first or second: (1) pre-commit a positive control symmetric to the specificity gate — a deliberately capability-destroyed checkpoint (train the same LoRA with the behavioral replay term removed, a high-LR scramble, or ablation of a random high-rank subspace at matched budget / adapter norm matched to the honesty run) on which the paired MC rule MUST fire (delta >= PAIR_DROP), else the battery is inadmissible for any DECOUPLED verdict (VOID_COUPLING__battery_insensitive), written into the frozen --calibrate receipt; (2) make DECOUPLED an earned verdict rather than the complement of COUPLED — per seed, call 'decoupled' only if the paired delta's upper confidence bound clears the bar (delta + 1.5*SE_paired < PAIR_DROP, an equivalence-style criterion), with the middle band scored 'undetermined' (counts toward PARTIAL); (3) if neither is adopted, demote the frozen DECOUPLED branch string from 'read_neq_write_BROKEN' to a bounded form ('no paired MC cost >= 0.15 detected at this battery's resolution') so the shipped claim matches what the instrument and test can carry.

findings.title: Specificity gate is estimated on read-intact pairs but the verdict fires at read-crossed points; attempt 2's own receipts show the fire rate covaries with read status, so the gate can certify an unearned COUPLED
findings.claim: The gate estimates the paired rule's false-fire rate ONLY on pairs where the accumulate read is INTACT (private-13 >= 0.70 AND bit), with no condition on the control read. The verdict point is conditioned the opposite way on BOTH arms: accumulate read CROSSED (< 0.70) AND control read intact (>= 0.70). If the battery co-fluctuates with the read via shared checkpoint-level perturbation-magnitude drift (the residual channel-sharing the design itself concedes: 'LoRA on attention+MLP can still move letter logits indirectly'), then conditioning on a crossing selects high-perturbation checkpoints where the MC aggregate is also depressed for non-capability reasons: the fire rate measured on the low-perturbation (read-intact) stratum systematically underestimates the fire rate on the crossing stratum. The double selection at r* (accumulate read on a downward fluctuation, control read on an upward one) plus regression to the mean additionally biases the paired delta fixed-minus-accumulate toward COUPLED in a way the gate structurally cannot see. Attempt 2's receipts quantify exactly this covariation: the break rule fired on 8 of 8 read-BROKEN accumulate checkpoints versus 37/52 = 0.7115 read-intact, and on the control arm 4/6 read-broken vs 31/54 = 0.5741 intact. A gate reading, say, 0.10 on intact pairs is compatible with near-certain firing at crossing points, so the run can pass its own admissibility rail and return a COUPLED that is still instrument-driven — attempt 2's failure moved one level up, from shared tokens to shared perturbation-magnitude noise, and the new rail does not see it.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 96-101: 'if not p.get("bit") or p["private13"] < SURVIVAL: continue' — every crossing checkpoint is excluded from the specificity denominator and no control-read condition is applied; lines 109-139 (_seed_outcome) apply paired_broke exactly and only at a crossing checkpoint, with r* requiring acc private13 < SURVIVAL AND fixed private13 >= SURVIVAL (lines 118-123). PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 56-73 (Fix 2, gate defined solely on read-intact pairs), lines 58-62 ('where the accumulate arm's read is INTACT ... nothing has been removed'), lines 133-136 (names only 'indirect drift below the gate's resolution', never the selection asymmetry between the gate's population and the verdict's population). capability_battery_mc.py lines 13-14: the indirect path is conceded. RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 31-48: accumulate 45/60 fired overall but 37/52 on read-intact => 8/8 on read-broken; control 35/60 overall, 31/54 intact => 4/6 broken; matched-step table lines 54-68: seeds 2/3/4 control read and control battery depressed together — the joint-wobble null is live, not hypothetical.
findings.fix: Before freezing: (a) condition the gate pairs symmetrically with the verdict — also require the control read >= 0.70 in the gate population, matching the r* pair on that arm; (b) pre-commit a read-stratified diagnostic with teeth: among read-intact pairs, report the paired MC delta stratified by accumulate read level (e.g. intact-low 0.70-0.78 vs intact-high >= 0.78) and require the low-stratum fire rate to also sit <= SPEC_MAX, or report Spearman(read, paired delta) with a pre-committed bar — so read-correlated firing voids the battery even when the pooled intact rate passes; (c) at minimum, name this conditioning mismatch in the inference-bounds section instead of only 'indirect drift below the gate's resolution'. This costs no GPU time — it is a re-cut of pairs the gate already computes.

findings.title: The gate's premise 'read intact = nothing has been removed' is false: high-dose recovered checkpoints contaminate the specificity null pool, so the most natural coupled world VOIDs the battery with a wrong stated cause and COUPLED becomes structurally near-unreachable
findings.claim: Erasure dose (accumulated rank) grows monotonically from step 25 regardless of whether the read is above 0.70, and the dose run showed the read RECOVERS above the survival bar after r* (seed 0: 0.7346 at rank 10, 0.8369 at rank 22; seed 1: 0.7465 at rank 10, 0.8037 at rank 14). Those rank-10-24, read-intact checkpoints — the highest-dose checkpoints of the run — enter the specificity gate's denominator, which the prereg justifies with 'nothing has been removed, so a paired capability drop there is instrument noise, not an attack price'. If the coupling hypothesis is true in its most natural form — price scales with accumulated rank and persists or grows while the read transiently recovers — the paired rule fires hardest precisely on those high-rank read-intact pairs, the pooled rate exceeds SPEC_MAX 0.15, and the run returns VOID_COUPLING__battery_lacks_dose_specificity with the frozen interpretation 'the MC channel is ALSO noise-dominated'. That claim about the instrument would be wrong: the firing would be dose-response, not noise. Net effect: COUPLED is reachable only in worlds where capability damage is transient and co-located with the read's crossing; DECOUPLED is reachable whenever the battery is quiet. The two terminal verdicts have asymmetric reachability, and the asymmetry points at the strong-break claim.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 59-61 ('the accumulate arm's read is INTACT ... nothing has been removed, so a paired capability drop there is instrument noise, not an attack price') and lines 64-66 (VOID interpretation 'the MC channel is ALSO noise-dominated'). coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 90-106 (specificity_gate pools ALL read-intact pairs regardless of erased_rank; lines 97-99 include every bit, read-intact accumulate checkpoint at any rank). RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md lines 42-47: 'After r_star both accumulate arms RECOVER: seed 0 climbs back to 0.7346 at rank 10 and reaches 0.8369 at rank 22 ... seed 1 climbs to 0.7465 at rank 10' — high-dose, read-intact checkpoints that enter the specificity pool.
findings.fix: Before freezing, restrict the gate population to LOW-DOSE / pre-crossing read-intact pairs (e.g. checkpoints before the seed's first crossing, erased_rank <= the rank at first crossing minus one refit, or erased_rank <= the fixed arm's rank), where 'no attack price yet' is defensible — or stratify the gate's firing rate by erased_rank and pre-commit both branches' interpretations: flat-in-rank firing among read-intact pairs -> instrument noise (VOID as written); rank-increasing firing -> reportable dose-response evidence OF coupling routed to a distinct outcome (COUPLED_dose_graded candidate or a named PARTIAL), not to the battery-inadmissibility VOID. Rewrite the 'nothing has been removed' rationale, which is factually wrong for recovered checkpoints.

findings.title: Dose-attributability of r* is a single unbuffered comparison of two SE-0.06 reads scanned over ~12 checkpoints, with no multiplicity control — noise manufactures admissible crossings
findings.claim: r* is the first checkpoint where accumulate read < 0.70 AND control read >= 0.70, evaluated point-by-point over roughly 12-13 post-bite checkpoints per seed. Each read carries SE ~0.06 (EVAL n=66), and every crossing in the program's history sits within ~1 SE of the bar (0.671, 0.6756 vs 0.70; control non-attributable values 0.6811, 0.6571, 0.635). When both arms' true reads hover near 0.70, the AND-condition is satisfied by two independent ~1-SE fluctuations at some checkpoint with high probability across the scan, so a noise excursion gets promoted to a dose-attributable r* and the whole verdict is then scored at a regression-to-the-mean point. The prereg discloses thin margins ('crossings within ~1 SE of the bar remain thin-margin and are reported as such') but the decision rule takes no margin and applies no scan correction — reporting does not gate. Combined with the DECOUPLED-by-default rule this makes the BREAK verdict the modal outcome of a no-dose-effect world.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 78-81 (r* definition, pointwise), line 129 ('per-checkpoint AUROC SE ~ 0.06 — crossings within ~1 SE of the bar remain thin-margin and are reported as such'); coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 118-123 (bare comparisons p['private13'] < SURVIVAL and fixed >= SURVIVAL, no margin); RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 54-60 (all five r* reads 0.6295-0.6756 and three control reads 0.635-0.6811, everything within ~1 SE of 0.70).
findings.fix: Before freezing, require attributability with margin or persistence: e.g. control read minus accumulate read >= 1 SE (0.06) at the matched step, or the accumulate crossing must hold on two consecutive checkpoints, or the control must be intact at the crossing step AND its neighbors. Pre-commit whichever is chosen; disclose in the prereg that the pointwise rule was rejected for its scan multiplicity.

findings.title: The VOID_crossing_not_dose_attributable exclusion is direction-correlated: in attempt 2's observed world it removes exactly the three decoupled-direction seeds and shrinks the majority denominator
findings.claim: Walking attempt 2's world through the attempt-3 rule: seeds 2, 3, 4 crossed at steps where the control read was also below 0.70 (0.6811, 0.6571, 0.635), so their crossings are non-attributable; absent a later attributable crossing each becomes VOID_crossing_not_dose_attributable and leaves the denominator. Those three seeds' paired MC-context deltas are -0.0417, 0.0, 0.0 — all on the decoupled side of the bar — which is not a coincidence: a step where the control read crossed is a step of heavy shared drift where the control battery is also depressed, so the paired delta (control minus accumulate) is small. The exclusion therefore systematically removes decoupled-leaning seeds AND lowers the majority bar: with 2 exclusions, COUPLED can issue from 2 of 5 seeds (n_adm=3, 2 > 1.5). In the exact 3-exclusion attempt-2 world the run lands n_adm=2 -> VOID_COUPLING__underpowered — honest, but it means the design likely cannot return any coupling verdict in the one world the program has already observed, and the boundary between 'no verdict' and 'COUPLED from a 2-seed rump' is one seed's noisy control read.
findings.evidence: RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 54-64 (matched-step table: seeds 2/3/4 control reads 0.6811/0.6571/0.635; control battery 0.7917/0.6667/0.9167 vs accumulate 0.8333/0.6667/0.9167 => deltas -0.0417/0.0/0.0); PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 82-83 (excluded from the majority) and line 102 (strict majority over admissible seeds); coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 148-149, 158-160 (admissible excludes non-attributable; n_adm < 3 -> underpowered).
findings.fix: Before freezing: (a) pre-commit a sensitivity disclosure with teeth — the excluded seeds' paired deltas at their (non-attributable) crossings must be reported next to the verdict, and if their direction-majority contradicts the issued majority the verdict demotes to PARTIAL; or (b) require n_admissible >= 4 for a COUPLED/DECOUPLED majority when any seed was excluded for non-attributability. Either keeps the exclusion from both silencing one direction and shrinking the bar it must clear.

findings.title: Specificity gate is weakly powered at its own floor: a noise-dominated instrument with true fire rate 0.25 passes 22.5% of the time at MIN_SPEC_PAIRS=20, and pooling ignores serial correlation and unequal per-seed pair counts
findings.claim: The gate is a point-estimate threshold (rate <= 0.15) with no confidence bound. At the frozen minimum of 20 pairs it admits fired <= 3; binomially, an instrument with true false-fire rate 0.25 passes with probability 0.225, rate 0.20 passes with 0.411, and even 0.30 passes with 0.107. The floor of 20 binds exactly in heavy-crossing worlds (few read-intact pairs), i.e. precisely when the verdict-carrying seeds are most exposed. Two further effects erode even these numbers: (a) pairs within a seed are consecutive checkpoints of one training run, so they are serially correlated and the effective sample is smaller than the count; (b) pooling across seeds lets a stable surviving seed's many intact pairs dilute the pooled rate below SPEC_MAX while the crossing seeds' own fire rates exceed it — the verdict then rests on per-seed instruments the pooled gate never individually admitted. At realistic pair counts (~50, as in attempt 2's 52 intact checkpoints) the binomial power is adequate (true 0.25 passes only 3.3%), so the defect is concentrated at and near the frozen floor.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py lines 37-38 (SPEC_MAX = 0.15, MIN_SPEC_PAIRS = 20); coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 90-106 (pooled across all seeds, raw rate, no interval) and lines 161-164 (gate branches). Computed: P(X<=3 | n=20, p=0.25) = 0.2252; P(X<=3 | n=20, p=0.20) = 0.4114; P(X<=3 | n=20, p=0.30) = 0.1071; P(X<=7 | n=52, p=0.25) = 0.0332.
findings.fix: Before freezing, either raise MIN_SPEC_PAIRS to ~40-50 (attempt-2 geometry supplies ~52-65 pairs, so this costs nothing in the expected world), or replace the point-estimate rule with an upper-confidence-bound rule (e.g. Wilson 90% upper bound of the fire rate <= SPEC_MAX), and add a per-seed disclosure of fire rates so a pooled pass cannot mask a hot verdict-carrying seed.
