findings.title: Prereg's '>= 3 survivors / MC selection ok' admissibility conjunct is unenforced on the scored path: MIN_DISJOINT is decorative, the calibration receipt is written even when ok=False, and base_model is never verified
findings.claim: The prereg's frozen verdict branch 1 requires 'MC selection ok AND clean selected aggregate >= 0.80 AND each selected sub-task >= 0.70, else VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed', with Fix 1 defining selection-ok as 'require >= 3 survivors (else VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed)'. The harness never implements the 'selection ok' conjunct anywhere on the scored path: battery_guard() checks only 'if not selected: return False' plus the two floors — a selection of 1 or 2 survivors passes. run_calibrate() writes coupling_v3_mc_selected.json UNCONDITIONALLY, before the ok flag is acted on (only main() signals via process exit code 2, which persists nothing). run_real() gates on file existence alone, loads selmeta['selected_disjoint_mc'], never reads selmeta['ok'], never re-checks len(selected) >= CBMC.MIN_DISJOINT, and never verifies selmeta['base_model'] against ctx['MODEL']. So a 1-2 survivor selection would score the run on a battery the prereg declares inadmissible — an unearned verdict forcing a VOID later — and the VOID branch frozen for exactly that contingency can never fire for it. The --dry suite exercises guard_verdict only with boolean inputs and never tests a 2-survivor selection, so the gap is invisible to the 15/15 dry receipt. Triggering is plausible: the 0.90 floor is effectively 15/16 per sub-task, and ORTH_LAST_MC/CONTAINS_MC/ALPHA_MC are spelling/tokenizer-hard for a 1.5B model. (Attempt 2's coupling_confirm.py / capability_battery.py lines 159-166 has the same inherited gap, but prereg3 re-freezes the >=3 requirement explicitly.)

C:\Users\heyzo\clawd\styxx\papers\calib-poison-general\coupling_confirm_v3.py line 323: SELECTION_CACHE.write_text(json.dumps(sel, ...)) with no ok gate; lines 332-337: run_real gates on file existence (line 332), selmeta = json.loads(SELECTION_CACHE.read_text(...)); selected = selmeta['selected_disjoint_mc'] — 'ok' never consulted; line 357: guard_battery = bool(CBMC.battery_guard(clean_mc, selected)); main() line 547: exit code 2 only. run_dry lines 425-530: guard_verdict exercised only with boolean inputs, no 2-survivor case. findings.fix: Before freeze: make the scored path enforce the prereg conjunct — e.g. guard_battery = bool(len(selected) >= CBMC.MIN_DISJOINT and selmeta.get('ok') is True and selmeta.get('base_model') == ctx['MODEL'] and CBMC.battery_guard(clean_mc, selected)) — or add 'and len(selected) >= MIN_DISJOINT' inside battery_guard itself; have run_calibrate refuse to write the receipt (or write it with an _INVALID suffix) when ok is False; and add a dry/selftest case where a 2-survivor selection yields VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed.

findings.title: PAIR_DROP 0.15 was set with attempt-2's paired deltas in hand (max 0.0417) on byte-identical dynamics and the same five seeds — the run is pre-decided toward DECOUPLED and the prereg does not say so
findings.claim: The prereg discloses applying the new paired rule to attempt 2's r* cells (deltas -0.0833, 0.0417, -0.0417, 0.0, 0.0) and frames the result as vindication of the paired contrast ('the old data never shows a paired capability price anywhere near 0.15'). But attempt 3 inherits the training loop verbatim, the same model, the same seeds {0,1,2,3,4}, the same split, and even carries a reproduction guard expecting matched reads to 0.02 — so the read-side dynamics are a known re-run. The threshold was therefore frozen at 3.6x the largest paired delta ever observed in the very dynamics about to be reproduced, and 1.65x the only observed knowledge price (0.0909). Unless the new MC channel behaves qualitatively differently from every capability readout so far, the expected verdict at freeze time is DECOUPLED, and the prereg presents the deltas as 'context only' without stating that implication. This is not post-result tuning of THIS run, but it is threshold selection informed by the same-seed data the run will replay, tilted toward the strong-break branch.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 88-91: 'Applied to attempt 2's own r* cells for context ... the five paired deltas are -0.0833, 0.0417, -0.0417, 0.0, 0.0 ... never shows a paired capability price anywhere near 0.15'. Lines 32-36: 'copies coupling_confirm.py's training loop verbatim ... same model Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct, seeds {0,1,2,3,4}'. Lines 122-123: reproduction guard, |delta| <= 0.02 vs attempt-2 receipt. RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md line 28: knowledge_drop 0.0909 on the coupled seed.
findings.fix: Add an explicit expected-outcome disclosure to the prereg: 'under reproduced attempt-2 dynamics and a channel-agnostic price, the anticipated verdict is DECOUPLED; the run is informative only if the MC channel diverges from the T/F channel.' Better: derive PAIR_DROP from a pre-committed minimum effect of interest with a stated power target (e.g. 80% per-seed power at the observed 0.09 price implies a bar near 0.09 with more items to shrink SE), or enlarge the item banks so a 0.15 bar has real power. If the bar stays at 0.15, the DECOUPLED branch must not carry the 'read_neq_write_BROKEN' headline (see the verdict-string finding).

findings.title: Content-label token collision in VOWEL_MC / CASE_MC / ALPHA_MC: option contents are themselves A-D-adjacent letter tokens, so the readout cannot distinguish label-answering from content-answering
findings.claim: In three of seven gating sub-tasks the option STRINGS are single letters, sometimes literally A, B, C, or D. The scorer reads argmax over the label tokens A/B/C/D, so a model that answers with the content letter instead of the label letter is scored by accident. CASE_MC is worst: the correct CONTENT is always a letter, and answering it directly is the most natural completion ('uppercase form of b?' -> 'B'), yet for 12 of 16 items the content letter differs from its label (e.g., content B at label A is scored as wrong via the label-B group). The honesty LoRA trains one-word-answer formatting on a near-identical instruction template ('Answer with exactly one word: True or False' vs 'Answer with exactly one letter: A, B, C, or D'), so fine-tuning plausibly shifts label-vs-content answering style. The paired contrast subtracts arm-common shifts and the specificity gate bounds resulting noise, so this cannot silently flip a verdict — but it injects fine-tune-movable measurement variance into exactly the channel the design claims is isolated, spending specificity-gate headroom on an avoidable confound.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py line 122: ("Which of the following is the uppercase form of the letter 'b'?", ["B", "D", "P", "R"], "A") — the correct content string 'B' sits at label A while token 'B' is the label group for the WRONG option 'D'. Line 104: VOWEL_MC options ["A", "B", "D", "F"] are literal label-alphabet tokens. Lines 157-174: ALPHA_MC options are all single letters, many in {A,B,C,D}. letter_token_ids (lines 203-213) builds groups from bare/space-prefixed 'A'..'D' with no disambiguation from option content. mc_prompt (lines 195-200) renders both channels into the same next-token position.
findings.fix: Before freeze, replace single-letter option contents with multi-character renderings that tokenize away from the label alphabet (e.g., quote the content: A) 'B'  B) 'D' — or spell 'the letter B'), or restrict letter-content items to option letters outside {A,B,C,D}. Re-run --selftest; no other change needed.

findings.title: The 'no direct gradient / shares no tokens' isolation claim is mechanistically overstated: the honesty loss's log_softmax partition function gives every letter logit a direct gradient, and letter-group/TF-group disjointness is asserted but never asserted at runtime
findings.claim: The behavioral term is -logsumexp(logp[T-or-F ids]) with logp = log_softmax over the full vocabulary; the normalizer term's gradient with respect to EVERY logit — including A/B/C/D — is softmax(z)_i > 0, so letter logits are pushed down by the training objective at every training step (only the frozen unembedding ROWS receive no gradient, because lm_head is outside the LoRA target modules). The battery's isolation premise therefore rests on (a) argmax-over-letters being invariant to near-uniform suppression and (b) the empirical gate — not on the absence of gradient the docs assert. Separately, the code never checks that the four letter groups are disjoint from tids/fids (or from each other): 'shares no tokens with the training target' is a tokenizer-dependent claim about Qwen2.5's BPE that is currently ungrounded by any receipt, and the RESULT doc will quote it.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py lines 10-11: 'The letter tokens receive no direct gradient from the honesty objective.' PREREG lines 43-44: 'the channel shares no tokens with the training target.' coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 256-259: 'logp = torch.log_softmax(logits, dim=-1); losses = [-torch.logsumexp(logp[i, ...' — the softmax normalizer couples all vocab logits into the loss. letter_token_ids (capability_battery_mc.py lines 203-213) and run_real (coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 287-288, 299) build letter groups and tids/fids with no disjointness assertion between them.
findings.fix: Two-line fix in _load_base_and_clean or letter_token_ids: assert set().union(*groups).isdisjoint(set(tids) | set(fids)) and assert pairwise-disjoint groups, and record the resolved token ids in the calibration receipt. Reword the docstring/prereg sentence to what is true and sufficient: 'no trainable unembedding parameters and no selective per-letter gradient; the objective suppresses all non-T/F logits uniformly through the softmax normalizer, which argmax-within-letters is invariant to; residual differential movement is what the specificity gate measures.'

findings.title: No per-pair 'points' receipt list for the specificity rate; prereg calls the gate's record a 'ledger' and the cache is named _cells.jsonl — certifier-vocabulary friction for the future RESULT doc
findings.claim: The specificity block in the result JSON stores only aggregate counts (n_pairs, n_fired, rate, mean/sd); the per-pair records (seed, step, acc read, fixed read, delta, fired) are never written anywhere, so the verdict-gating rate is re-derivable only by re-running gate logic over curves. Under the house certifier rule that per-cell receipt lists grounding numbers must be named 'points' (and that 'cells'/'rows'/'ledger'-named lists cannot ground), the RESULT doc's central number (fired/pairs on a trigger-vocab line) has no conforming receipt list to point at. The prereg additionally pre-commits the word 'ledger' for exactly this record, steering the future RESULT toward banned vocabulary.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 103-106: specificity_gate returns only {'n_pairs', 'n_fired', 'rate', 'max_allowed', 'min_pairs', 'mean_delta', 'sd_delta'} — the deltas list (line 101) is aggregated and discarded, no per-pair list. PREREG line 124: 'The specificity gate's full ledger: n_pairs, n_fired, rate, mean/sd of read-intact deltas.' Line 64 of coupling_confirm_v3.py: CELLS_CACHE = HERE / 'coupling_confirm_v3_cells.jsonl'.
findings.fix: Before freeze, add a 'points' key to the specificity dict: a list of per-pair records {seed, step, acc_read, fixed_read, delta, fired}; change the prereg word 'ledger' to 'points list'. Leave the cache filename if the certifier only inspects the result JSON, but renaming to _points.jsonl is free insurance.

findings.title: The 'pure noise fires on the order of 7 percent' margin claim counts only item-level binomial noise; attempt 2's own checkpoint dispersion exceeds the binomial ceiling, so 7% is a floor, not an estimate
findings.claim: The prereg derives 7% from the worst-case binomial SE at 48 items (0.0722 per checkpoint, 0.102 paired; 0.15/0.102 = 1.47 sigma -> 7.1% one-sided). But attempt 2's receipts show per-checkpoint battery SDs of 0.1399 (accumulate) and 0.1111 (control) — above the binomial maximum of 0.0722 — meaning checkpoint-level drift, not item sampling, dominates the dispersion, and pairing two independent training runs does not cancel run-specific drift. If MC drift resembles T/F drift the true noise fire rate is several times 7% and the gate fails; the prereg's 'the bar is passable with margin by a sane instrument' therefore rests on an unmeasured quantity (MC checkpoint drift), while presenting an item-noise-only calculation as the design margin.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 69-73 ('per-checkpoint aggregate SE is at most 0.072, the paired-difference SE at most 0.102, so PAIR_DROP = 0.15 sits at roughly 1.5 paired SEs and pure noise fires on the order of 7 percent of pairs'); RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 75-78 (accumulate mean 0.8035 sd 0.1399; control mean 0.8625 sd 0.1111 — both SDs above the 48-item binomial ceiling 0.0722).
findings.fix: Reword the margin paragraph: state that 7% is the item-noise floor, that attempt 2's T/F channel showed checkpoint-level dispersion 1.5-2x the binomial ceiling, and that the gate exists precisely because the MC channel's checkpoint drift is unknown before the run. No threshold change required.

findings.title: 16-item quantization makes every nominal threshold operatively different from its printed value — selection floor 0.90 is effectively 15/16 = 0.9375, sub-task floor 0.70 is 12/16 = 0.75, and PAIR_DROP 0.15 is 0.1667 at the minimum 3-subtask selection — nowhere disclosed
findings.claim: On 16-item sub-tasks, accuracy takes values in multiples of 1/16 and aggregates over k selected sub-tasks in multiples of 1/(16k), so every frozen threshold has an operative value stricter than its printed one. Selection: DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN = 0.90 is effectively 15/16 = 0.9375 (at most one miss) and CLEAN_SUBTASK_FLOOR = 0.70 is effectively 12/16 = 0.75 — materially higher bars that raise the realistic risk of fewer than 3 survivors (interacting with the unenforced >=3-survivor guard finding). Verdict rule: at k=3 no achievable paired delta lies in (0.1458, 0.1667), so 'delta >= 0.15' is operationally 'delta >= 0.1667' — 1.63 paired SEs, noise fire 5.1% rather than the stated 7.1%; at k=4 the effective bar is 0.15625 (grid 1/64); only at k=5 is 0.15 exactly achievable (12/80). The same shift applies to the specificity gate's expected fire rate. The thresholds are frozen on paper but their operative severity varies with the data-dependent number of calibration survivors — worth pre-declaring so a post-run reader cannot read the k-dependence as tuning or the floors as more forgiving than they are. (The 4-decimal rounding itself is harmless: all k/16 sub-task scores are exact at 4 decimals, and no achievable delta sits within rounding distance of 0.15 at k=3 or 4.)
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py line 36 (PAIR_DROP = 0.15), lines 39, 42 (DISJOINT_FLOOR_CLEAN = 0.90, CLEAN_SUBTASK_FLOOR = 0.70) over banks of exactly 16 items (lines 48-175), lines 260-268 (paired_broke: delta = round(agg_fixed - agg_acc, 4); >= PAIR_DROP); PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md line 48 ('keep sub-tasks the CLEAN base clears at 0.90', no mention of the one-miss bar) and lines 69-71 (minimum selection 3 x 16 = 48 items). The selftest's own synthetic base (line 331) uses 0.9375 values, implicitly acknowledging the quantization without stating it. Computed: 7/48 = 0.1458, 8/48 = 0.1667; z = 0.1667/0.1021 = 1.63 -> 5.1% noise fire; 4 survivors: 10/64 = 0.15625.
findings.fix: Add sentences to the prereg's Fix 2/Fix 3 sections (and the battery docstring) freezing the effective bars: 'on 16 items the 0.90 selection floor is effectively 15/16 = 0.9375 (one miss) and the 0.70 sub-task floor is 12/16 = 0.75'; and the achievable-delta grid per selection size (1/48, 1/64, 1/80) with resulting effective PAIR_DROP bars (0.1667 / 0.15625 / 0.15). This makes the k-dependence disclosed rather than discovered post-result, and makes a <3-survivor calibration outcome recognized as anticipated rather than anomalous.

findings.title: Specificity VOIDs precede and can veto SURVIVES, a verdict the battery never gates
findings.claim: The frozen order places both specificity VOIDs (rate > SPEC_MAX; n_pairs < 20) before the all-survive branch. SURVIVES is determined entirely by the read never crossing — the MC battery plays no role in it — yet a noisy battery (or, per the ordering, an unmeasurable one) converts an all-survive world into VOID_COUPLING__battery_lacks_dose_specificity or VOID_COUPLING__specificity_unmeasurable, withholding a read-only conclusion because an instrument it does not use failed admissibility. Since an all-survive world maximizes read-intact pairs, the unmeasurable branch is unreachable there, but the noise branch is live. This costs an honest verdict rather than manufacturing one, so it is an inference/efficiency defect, not a validity defect — but the ordering is frozen, so it must be fixed now or never.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 100-101 (step 4 'The specificity gate ... both its VOIDs precede any coupling outcome' before step 5 SURVIVES); coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 161-166 (spec checks at lines 161-164 return before the all-survive check at line 165).
findings.fix: Before freezing, either move the SURVIVES branch ahead of the specificity gate (with a note that SURVIVES carries no capability claim), or keep the order and add a sentence to the prereg acknowledging that a battery-noise VOID deliberately suppresses even battery-independent verdicts, so the choice is visibly intentional.

findings.title: v3's audit() silently drops attempt 2's T/F aggregate lines: the 'verbatim copy / SOLE new delta / one added line' claim is false against the diff, and the prereg-committed channel-vs-channel overlay is left without a frozen T/F aggregation
findings.claim: The gradient/attack code path is byte-identical and the added MC measurement consumes no RNG (argmax under no_grad), so the seed-0/1 reproduction guard is safe — but audit() has two deltas, not one: the MC call (two lines + one return-dict key + the letter_groups parameter) is ADDED and attempt 2's bat['aggregate'] / bat['aggregate_adjacent'] lines are REMOVED. Consequences: (a) both the harness docstrings and the prereg claim 'verbatim copy ... ONE added audit line', wording the RESULT doc will repeat and that is falsifiable against the diff; (b) v3's stored T/F battery dicts lack the aggregate keys attempt 2's receipts carry — a shape change in the mechanism receipt the prereg commits to reporting as 'the channel-vs-channel overlay: T/F battery aggregate vs MC aggregate, both arms, every seed'; (c) no frozen T/F selection is loaded — the only T/F aggregate v3 computes is a console print over whatever DISJOINT_POOL keys are present, not attempt 2's frozen selection in coupling_confirm_disjoint_selected.json — so the overlay's T/F aggregation composition is left to post-hoc discretion at RESULT time. Per-subtask T/F scores ARE stored, so the overlay is derivable; only the aggregation choice is unfrozen and the provenance claim inaccurate. Gates nothing (report-only), so no verdict impact.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 25-26 ('copied verbatim from coupling_confirm.train_accumulating with ONE added audit line') and 179-180 ('Verbatim copy ... the SOLE new delta is the CBMC.measure_all_mc call'); PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md line 32 ('copies coupling_confirm.py's training loop verbatim') and lines 118-119 (overlay commitment). coupling_confirm.py lines 154-155: 'bat["aggregate"] = CB.aggregate(bat, selected); bat["aggregate_adjacent"] = ...' — absent from v3 audit() (lines 205-221, aggregates dropped at 211-213, per-subtask scores stored at line 218). v3 line 402: tf_agg computed as CB.aggregate(tail['battery'], [k for k in tail['battery'] if k in CB.DISJOINT_POOL]) — the full pool, print-only. Diff of coupling_confirm.py lines 117-206 vs coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 177-266: only divergences are inside audit(); everything from 'checkpoints = [audit(0)]' through 'opt.step()' is byte-identical.
findings.fix: Preferred: restore the two T/F aggregate lines in v3's audit(), computed over attempt 2's frozen T/F selection (load coupling_confirm_disjoint_selected.json) — this makes the copy claim true, the receipts shape-compatible, and the overlay directly comparable to attempt 2 without recomputing aggregates with off-receipt code. Also reword both docstrings and the prereg sentence to name the deltas exactly: 'training/attack path copied verbatim; audit() adds the MC measurement (two lines + one return key) and drops/restores the T/F aggregate lines'. If not restoring, add one prereg sentence freezing which T/F aggregation the overlay will use (e.g. 'attempt 2's frozen T/F selection') so the mechanism receipt is not assembled with post-hoc freedom.

findings.title: CONTAINS_MC has a longest-option cue: the correct option is strictly the longest in 8 of 16 items
findings.claim: Because rare letters (X, Z, Q, W) live in longer words, the gold in CONTAINS_MC is strictly the longest option in 8/16 items (saxophone, conquest, sandwich, blanket, blizzard, mixture, firewood, textbook), tied-longest in ~6, and strictly non-longest in only 2 (banjo, horizon). A degraded model falling back on a superficial pick-the-longest heuristic scores roughly 8/16 plus a quarter of the ties (~0.59) instead of chance 0.25 — the sub-task's floor under damage is inflated, compressing the dynamic range available to the paired contrast on this sub-task. The bias cannot grant a selection pass (0.59 is far below the 0.9375 effective floor) and the paired design applies it symmetrically to both arms, so it cannot flip a verdict on its own — but it makes CONTAINS_MC the least dose-sensitive bank if it survives selection.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py lines 85-101: gold word lengths vs distractor lengths, e.g. line 88 ('saxophone' 9 vs candle/ribbon/turtle 6), line 90 ('conquest' 8 vs marble/sunset/tiger 5-6), line 94 ('blizzard' 8 vs valley/needle/rocket 6), line 101 ('textbook' 8 vs evening/hammock/pencil 6-7). No other bank shows a comparable systematic length cue (checked ORTH_FIRST/ORTH_LAST/VOWEL/CASE/ANTONYM/ALPHA item-by-item).
findings.fix: No code change required for admissibility (selection and the specificity gate both police the consequence). If editing before freeze is cheap: swap 3-4 distractors for length-matched words containing no target letter (e.g. 'telephone', 'chandelier', 'boulevard' against 'saxophone'). Otherwise, one line in the RESULT doc's battery description noting the cue and that pairing neutralizes it.

findings.title: VOWEL_MC item 7 carries the contestable 'Y' distractor while the effective floor allows only one miss
findings.claim: Item 7 of VOWEL_MC ('Which of the following letters is a vowel?', options Y/E/Z/B, gold E) uses Y as a distractor. Under the file's ground truth (_VOWELS = AEIOU) exactly one option is correct, so the selftest passes — but 'Y is sometimes a vowel' is standard English pedagogy, and a model placing mass on the Y letter-token is not exhibiting a capability failure. Combined with the 15/16 effective selection floor (one-miss budget), this single contestable item can consume the entire miss budget for the sub-task at calibration, or add non-capability noise to checkpoint scores if VOWEL_MC is selected.
findings.evidence: capability_battery_mc.py line 110: ("Which of the following letters is a vowel?", ["Y", "E", "Z", "B"], "B") — the only appearance of Y as a vowel-question distractor in the bank; line 272: _VOWELS = set("AEIOU").
findings.fix: Before freeze, replace Y with any unambiguous consonant not already in the item (e.g. "W" is also semi-ambiguous — use "T": ["T", "E", "Z", "B"]). One-character change, removes the only defensibly-arguable gold in the seven gating banks.

findings.title: SPEC_MAX 0.15 admits an instrument whose false-COUPLED family rate is within ~2.5x of the null probability used to condemn attempt 2
findings.claim: SPEC_MAX and PAIR_DROP being the same 0.15 is not itself incoherent (a rate vs a delta), and a barely-admissible battery is not coin-flip per seed. But the prereg justifies the gate with a sane-instrument figure ('pure noise fires on the order of 7 percent of pairs') while the frozen bar admits batteries firing at up to 15% per read-intact pair. At the admissibility boundary, P(false COUPLED majority, >=3 of 5 seeds at per-seed 0.15) is approximately 0.027 — about 9x the ~0.003 implied by the advertised 7% figure, and only ~2.5x below the 0.0675 five-of-five null probability that the VOID doc cited as 'a verdict that arrives this cheaply ... is not evidence'. The prereg never computes the worst-admissible case, so the gate's real guarantee is weaker than the text implies.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 69-73: 'pure noise fires on the order of 7 percent of pairs' as the justification for SPEC_MAX 0.15. capability_battery_mc.py lines 36-38: PAIR_DROP 0.15, SPEC_MAX 0.15. RESULT_B2_coupling_confirm_VOID_2026_07_16.md lines 72-74: 'the probability of every seed reading as coupled is 0.0675. A verdict that arrives this cheaply under a dose-free null is not evidence'.
findings.fix: Either tighten SPEC_MAX toward the advertised sane-instrument rate (e.g. 0.10), or add one sentence to the prereg's inference bounds stating the worst-admissible-case arithmetic: at spec rate = 0.15 the false-COUPLED majority probability is ~0.027, and any COUPLED verdict from a battery passing near the boundary must be reported with that number attached.

findings.title: Verdict string and pre-committed scope overclaim the DECOUPLED branch: 'read_neq_write_BROKEN' with 'capability held' rather than 'no price visible to this battery', with no deafness-residual caveat in inference bounds
findings.claim: The inference-bounds section scopes the false-COUPLED residual explicitly ('does NOT prove the MC channel is causally isolated ... indirect drift below the gate's resolution remains possible and is named here') but contains no mirror sentence for the false-DECOUPLED residual — that a price below PAIR_DROP 0.15, or invisible to a 3-7 sub-task ceiling-trivial letter-MC proxy, remains possible. Meanwhile the frozen branch string is DECOUPLED__read_neq_write_BROKEN_by_accumulation_paired_1p5B and the pre-committed scope reads 'with capability held at the paired values'. 'Held' is a positive assertion about capability; what the instrument licenses is 'no paired drop >= 0.15 on the selected MC battery'. Since the verdict string is frozen code, a future RESULT doc inherits the overclaim verbatim.
findings.evidence: PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 103-107: verdict string and scope 'with capability held at the paired values'; lines 133-136: battery-proxy caveat plus specificity-residual caveat, no sensitivity-residual caveat. coupling_confirm_v3.py line 168: the frozen string.
findings.fix: Amend the pre-committed scope sentence (in the prereg, before freeze) to: 'with no capability price at or above the paired bar of 0.15 visible to the selected MC battery' and add one inference-bounds sentence naming the deafness residual symmetric to the isolation residual. If the positive control from the fatal sensitivity finding is adopted, the string may keep BROKEN conditional on that control passing; otherwise rename the branch (e.g. DECOUPLED__no_price_visible_to_MC_paired_1p5B).

findings.title: Capability price is gated only at the first dose-attributable crossing — the mildest dose of a transient phenomenon — biasing the per-seed call toward decoupled
findings.claim: r* is `attributable[0]`: the smallest-rank crossing. The dose run established the crossing is transient (read dips at rank 8 for one checkpoint, recovers by rank 10-14) — so the verdict samples capability at the single weakest point of the attack, where any dose-graded price would be smallest. Paired deltas at every matched step are reported but gate nothing, so a price that grows with rank after r* cannot move the verdict; combined with the sensitivity findings this is a third independent tilt of the per-seed call toward decoupled. Inherited from the dose prereg's first-crossing definition, so arguably by design — flagged because attempt 3 upgrades the per-seed call into a potential read-neq-write break claim, which raises the cost of the bias.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py line 131: `pt = attributable[0]`. RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL_2026_07_14.md lines 42-47: 'The break is transient. After r_star both accumulate arms RECOVER ... The verdict is defined at the FIRST crossing per the frozen prereg'. PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md lines 120-121: full paired-delta dose-response is 'Reported, no bar'.
findings.fix: Pre-commit a reported (non-gating) secondary statistic with an interpretation rule: the maximum dose-attributable paired delta across ALL attributable crossings per seed, disclosed alongside the r* delta; if the max exceeds PAIR_DROP while the r* delta does not, the seed's decoupled call must be flagged as first-crossing-specific in the RESULT doc rather than presented as 'capability held'.

findings.title: VOID_COUPLING__no_calibration is a returnable verdict string absent from the frozen verdict set
findings.claim: run_real can return verdict 'VOID_COUPLING__no_calibration', which appears nowhere in the prereg's frozen verdict list and is not exercised by --dry. It fires only when the operator skips --calibrate, and it blocks rather than grants (safe direction), but it is an out-of-prereg verdict string in the result JSON namespace.
findings.evidence: coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 332-334: `return {"verdict": "VOID_COUPLING__no_calibration", ...}`. PREREG_B2_coupling_paired_2026_07_16.md 'Frozen verdict' section (lines 92-112) enumerates the verdict set; no_calibration is not in it (inherited from coupling_confirm.py lines 269-271, where it was likewise unpreregistered).
findings.fix: Add one line to the prereg's artifacts/verdict section acknowledging the operational precondition verdict ('scored run without a committed calibration receipt returns VOID_COUPLING__no_calibration and is not a scored outcome'), or rename it to a non-verdict error field so the verdict namespace stays exactly the frozen set.

findings.title: Docstring/prereg overstate the selftest: '490 checks' are not 490 gold recomputations — only 120 are
findings.claim: Both the battery docstring ('`--selftest` recomputes EVERY gold from ground truth ... asserts exactly one option satisfies each item's predicate', lines 21-23) and the prereg ('`--selftest` recomputes every gold from ground truth (490 checks)', line 47) attach the 490 figure to gold recomputation. Verified by running the selftest and counting the receipt: 490/490 pass, but only 120 checks are ':label' gold recomputations (one per item, all 120 items); the rest are structural (count, balance, 4-distinct-options, gold-in-ABCD, exactly-one-correct) plus 26 synthetic logic checks on select/guard/paired rules. The selftest itself is NOT circular — banks are hand-written literals and the predicates derive truth independently (question-text parse + string ops, Python str.upper, min() for ALPHA_MC, int multiplication for MUL_MC; the only same-file ground truth is the 16-entry _ANTONYM map, whose pairs are standard antonyms) — and the reviewer independently confirmed all 120 golds and the 4/4/4/4 (2/2/2/2 for MUL_MC) balance by hand. The claim is true in substance, wrong in arithmetic.
findings.evidence: Selftest run output: 'capability_battery_mc selftest: all_ok=True (490/490)'; parsing capability_battery_mc_selftest_INVALID.json: 120 checks end in ':label', 26 are non-item logic checks (select:*, guard:*, paired:*), remainder structural. capability_battery_mc.py lines 306-350 (_selftest), lines 21-23 (docstring); PREREG line 47.
findings.fix: Rephrase in both places to e.g. 'selftest: 490 checks, of which 120 recompute every gold from independent ground truth; the rest assert bank structure (counts, 4/4/4/4 balance, distinct options, exactly-one-correct) and the select/guard/paired rule logic'. Cheap now; in the RESULT doc a reviewer recounting the receipt will flag the discrepancy.

result.reason: Every factual claim checks out against the artifacts. capability_battery_mc.py lines 37-38 freeze SPEC_MAX=0.15 and MIN_SPEC_PAIRS=20; coupling_confirm_v3.py specificity_gate (lines 90-106) pools fired/n across all seeds as a raw point-estimate rate with no confidence bound and no per-seed rates, and the verdict branches (lines 161-164) pass the gate iff fired <= 3 at n=20 (strict > comparison, so rate exactly 0.15 passes). The binomial computations were independently recomputed and are correct: P(pass | true fire rate 0.25, n=20) = 0.2252, p=0.20 -> 0.4115, p=0.30 -> 0.1071, versus 0.033 at n=52 — so the gate's discriminating power collapses precisely at its own frozen floor. No frozen guard handles this: the prereg's Fix 2 power argument only estimates a sane instrument's expected noise fire rate (~7%), never the pass probability of a degraded instrument at MIN_SPEC_PAIRS, and its inference-bounds section names "drift below the gate's resolution" without quantifying that resolution. The serial-correlation point is a correct reading (intact pairs are consecutive audits of one training run, shrinking effective n below 20), and the pooling-dilution point is real (per-seed paired deltas are reported as receipts but nothing gates on them, so a hot verdict-carrying seed can ride a diluted pooled rate). The floor binds exactly in heavy-crossing worlds — the verdict-carrying ones. The error direction is fatal-adjacent: weak gate power admits a noise-dominated instrument, licensing a favourable verdict — the same failure mode that voided attempt 2. This is a pre-freeze review; the finding stands as major.

The claimed conditioning mismatch is real and verified in the code. coupling_confirm_v3.py specificity_gate (lines 96-101) filters gate pairs solely on the ACCUMULATE checkpoint being read-intact (bit AND private13 >= SURVIVAL) with no condition on the control checkpoint, while _seed_outcome (lines 118-123, 130-132) applies the paired rule only at points conditioned the opposite way (acc read < SURVIVAL AND control read >= SURVIVAL). The two arms are independently trained models, so pairing removes mean step-level training effects but not per-checkpoint state fluctuation; the r* double selection (acc on a downward read excursion, control on an upward one) biases fixed-minus-accumulate toward COUPLED if MC covaries with perturbation magnitude, and the gate — estimated on the disjoint low-perturbation stratum, with fixed-crossed pairs included (further lowering its measured deltas) — is structurally blind to it. The covariation is documented in the program's own receipts: RESULT_B2 VOID doc shows fire rate 8/8 on read-broken vs 37/52 (0.7115) on read-intact accumulate checkpoints and 4/6 vs 31/54 (0.5741) on the control arm. The residual indirect path into the MC channel is conceded in capability_battery_mc.py lines 13-14, and the prereg's inference bounds name only 'indirect drift below the gate's resolution', never the gate-vs-verdict population asymmetry. No frozen guard covers it: the dose-attributable requirement is itself the second half of the biased selection, and paired deltas at other steps are 'reported, no bar'. One minor evidence imprecision (seed 4's control battery at r* is 0.9167, not depressed, so joint read+battery depression holds cleanly for seeds 2-3 only) does not touch the core defect. A gate rate <= 0.15 on the intact stratum is compatible with much higher firing at crossing points, so an instrument-driven COUPLED can pass the rail — the finding stands as written.

Not refuted. The claimed defect exists exactly as described: coupling_confirm_v3.py _seed_outcome (lines 113-130) implements r* as bare pointwise comparisons (acc private13 < 0.70 AND matched-step control >= 0.70) scanned over ~13 checkpoints per seed, with no margin, persistence, or multiplicity control. The prereg (line 129) concedes SE ~0.06 and thin-margin crossings but only 'reports' them — nothing gates. The hovering-near-0.70 regime is empirically established by the attempt-2 VOID table (all five r* reads 0.6295-0.6756; control itself crossed in 3/5 seeds at 0.635-0.6811). No frozen guard covers this: the specificity gate polices only the paired capability rule's false-fire rate, not the crossing/attributability rule; VOID_crossing_not_dose_attributable requires ALL crossings to be non-attributable (one opposite-sign noise coincidence in ~12 chances defeats it); the bit gate is orthogonal; ERASURE=0.60 is defined but unused in the verdict path. A noise-promoted r* feeds a paired MC contrast with expected delta ~0, yielding 'decoupled' per seed, so a decoupled majority (the BREAK verdict, asserting dose-attributable removal at measured rank r*) is indeed the likely outcome of a no-dose-effect world — a wrong/unearned verdict, consistent with severity major. The finding's only imprecision ('DECOUPLED-by-default' — it is a strict majority, not a literal default) does not touch the substantive statistical defect. The house rule that a control arm's firing rate must gate a rule's own admissibility is applied to the capability rule but not to the crossing rule, which independently supports the finding.

The finding stands. Every code citation verifies: specificity_gate (coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 96-101) conditions only on the accumulate arm being read-intact (bit AND private13 >= 0.70) with no control-read condition, while _seed_outcome (lines 113-132) fires the verdict exclusively at points conditioned the opposite way on both arms (acc read < 0.70 AND fixed read >= 0.70). The prereg's Fix 2 (lines 58-67) freezes the gate on that intact-only population and the inference bounds (lines 134-136) name only 'indirect drift below the gate's resolution', never the gate-population vs verdict-population conditioning mismatch or the double selection at r*. capability_battery_mc.py lines 13-14 concede the indirect letter-logit channel, so the gate is the sole rail against it — and it estimates the fire rate on a systematically different (lower-perturbation) stratum than where the verdict fires. No frozen guard covers this: the dose-attributability requirement IS the double selection; per-step paired deltas are 'Reported, no bar'; the dry-run spec_noise case uses read-UNcorrelated firing so read-correlated firing is never exercised. The attempt-2 receipts corroborate covariation arithmetically (accumulate 8/8 fired on read-broken vs 37/52=0.7115 intact; control 4/6 vs 31/54=0.5741), though I note two weaknesses in the evidence as stated: the control-arm contrast is small-sample (n=6 broken) and not individually significant, and seed 4 in the matched-step table shows read depression WITHOUT battery depression (0.635 read, 0.9167 battery, no break), so 'seeds 2/3/4 depressed together' strictly holds only for seeds 2/3, and the covariation magnitudes come from a different rule and battery. These weaken the empirical corroboration but do not refute the structural defect, which exists in the unfrozen code exactly as claimed and can certify an unearned COUPLED while passing the gate. Severity 'fatal' is fair for a pre-freeze review; the proposed symmetric-conditioning/stratified re-cut costs no GPU time.

Every evidence claim verifies against the artifacts. (a) coupling_confirm_v3.py _verdict (lines ~157-171) contains only specificity-side VOIDs; per-seed outcome is literally '"coupled" if broke else "decoupled"' (line 133) — DECOUPLED is the pure complement of a failure-to-fire, with no positive control, no equivalence bound, and no sensitivity branch anywhere in harness or prereg. (b) capability_battery_mc.py measure_mc_subtask (lines 226-229) scores by argmax only — invariant to margin shrinkage without winner flips — over ceiling-trivial banks (selection floor 0.90, line 39; attempt 2's analogous selection landed all selected sub-tasks at 1.0 per the VOID doc lines 86/127-129); the specificity gate measures only false-fire rate on read-intact pairs, so a maximally deaf battery passes it with rate 0 — the gate structurally rewards insensitivity and cannot substitute for a sensitivity check. eval_knowledge (v3 line 208) reads tids/fids and is reported-only, so MC is the sole gating capability channel. (c) The statistics recompute correctly: PAIR_DROP 0.15 vs paired SE 0.102 is a ~1.47-sigma one-sided test (power 0.50 at the bar, ~0.31 at drop 0.10, ~0.16 at 0.05); P(DECOUPLED majority | shared true drop 0.10) ~ 0.82 and ~0.997 under pure noise — majority voting concentrates a shared sub-bar miss rather than adding power, so the prereg's own justification (lines 131-133) is backwards for this failure mode. Candidate refutations fail: the inference-bounds prose names the proxy scope and the isolation (false-COUPLED) residual but never the deafness (false-DECOUPLED) residual; the pre-committed 'capability held at the paired values' scope bounds the claim but the shipped verdict token is still read_neq_write_BROKEN framed as 'A BREAK'; the --dry coupled case exercises verdict arithmetic on synthetic curves, not instrument sensitivity. The defect exists, is not handled by any frozen guard, and rests on no misreading. Severity 'fatal' is fair pre-freeze: the design's default output in both a null world and a genuinely-priced-below-bar world is its most sensational claim.

All cited evidence verifies against the artifacts. coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 118-131 implement r* as a bare pointwise AND (acc private13 < 0.70, matched-step fixed >= 0.70) taking the first qualifying checkpoint over a ~13-checkpoint scan, with no margin, persistence, or multiplicity control. Prereg line 129 discloses SE ~0.06 thin margins but only as reporting — nothing gates. The near-bar regime is empirically the observed one: all five attempt-2 r* reads sit 0.6295-0.6756 and controls 0.635-0.7641 (VOID result lines 54-60), and the dose result itself concedes the fixed arm makes its own sub-threshold excursions and that rank attribution is "directional, not clean". The specificity gate (Fix 2) disciplines only the MC PAIR_DROP rule over read-intact pairs, not the read-crossing scan; VOID_crossing_not_dose_attributable is the pointwise rule under critique, not a guard against it. Since a spurious r* then defaults to "decoupled" ~93-96% under a no-effect null (0.15 bar vs ~0.07-0.10 paired SE), the majority rule amplifies the error toward an unearned DECOUPLED/BREAK verdict. The defect exists, is unhandled by any frozen guard, and rests on no misreading.

All evidence verifies against the artifacts. (1) specificity_gate (coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 90-106) pools every bit=True, read-intact matched-step pair with no erased_rank restriction, and train_accumulating (lines 226-234) grows rank unconditionally every refit, so post-recovery rank 10-24 checkpoints — the highest-dose points — enter the denominator. (2) The prereg rationale (lines 58-61: 'nothing has been removed, so a paired capability drop there is instrument noise, not an attack price') and the frozen VOID interpretation (lines 64-66: 'the MC channel is ALSO noise-dominated') are quoted accurately. (3) The dose run's recovery numbers (RESULT_B2_coupling_dose_PARTIAL lines 42-44: seed 0 = 0.7346 @ rank 10, 0.8369 @ rank 22; seed 1 = 0.7465 @ rank 10, 0.8037 @ rank 14) are verbatim, so contamination is guaranteed by the known trajectory shape, not hypothetical. No frozen guard handles it: the per-step paired-delta table is reported-no-bar and cannot alter the verdict, and the frozen ordering fires the VOID before any coupling outcome. The physics strengthens the finding — the rank-22 union strictly contains the rank-8 union, so a constraint-caused price at r* should persist at recovered checkpoints, and the harness's own dry-run spec_noise case (mc_low_everywhere, exactly the persistent dose-response shape) is pre-committed to route to VOID_COUPLING__battery_lacks_dose_specificity with the 'noise' cause. Pre-crossing read-intact pairs alone (~15 pooled, below MIN_SPEC_PAIRS 20) cannot carry the gate, so the denominator is dominated by high-dose recovered pairs. COUPLED is reachable only if the price vanishes while the constraint monotonically grows; DECOUPLED whenever the battery is quiet. The asymmetry and the wrong-stated-cause VOID both stand. Severity 'major' is apt: in the natural coupled world this produces a VOID whose frozen interpretation ('noise-dominated') would be factually wrong, forcing an erratum.

The claimed defect exists exactly as described. coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 118-123 implement r* as bare pointwise comparisons (acc private13 < 0.70 AND matched-step fixed >= 0.70) with no margin, no persistence, first-hit selection over ~12-13 post-bite checkpoints. Prereg line 129 discloses the ~0.06 SE but only as reporting ("reported as such") — no verdict branch takes a margin or scan correction. RESULT_VOID lines 54-60 confirm all five historical r* reads (0.6295-0.6756) and the three non-attributable control reads (0.635-0.6811) sit within ~1-1.2 SE of the bar, and the VOID doc itself calls the crossing "transient," so checkpoint-level fluctuation around 0.70 is empirically established in this setup. No frozen guard handles it: the specificity gate (Fix 2) covers only the MC battery's false-fire rate on read-intact pairs, not noise in the crossing/attributability determination; VOID_crossing_not_dose_attributable requires ALL crossings to be non-attributable, which under two ~SE-0.06 arms hovering near the bar (P~0.25 per checkpoint, ~12 checkpoints) almost never happens — an attributable crossing is manufactured per seed with probability near 1, and since the paired MC delta rarely reaches 0.15 (guaranteed by the specificity gate itself), each seed lands 'decoupled' and the majority yields the DECOUPLED break verdict. The bias is directionally coherent across seeds, so the 5-seed majority provides no protection. Minor imprecisions (only two of five reads quoted, 0.6295 is ~1.2 SE not 1, 'DECOUPLED-by-default' is loose for strict-majority-with-PARTIAL-fallback) do not touch the substance. The finding stands as a major pre-freeze defect.

Every load-bearing factual claim verifies against the artifacts. (a) No sensitivity control exists anywhere: coupling_confirm_v3.py _verdict (lines 142-171) has VOIDs only for no_bite, underpowered, specificity_unmeasurable, and battery_lacks_dose_specificity; DECOUPLED issues as the bare complement of failure-to-fire (line 133: '"coupled" if broke else "decoupled"'), and eval_knowledge (line 208, cited as 210 — immaterial) reads through the voided tids/fids channel and is reported-only, making the MC battery the sole gating channel. A deaf battery passes the specificity gate trivially (rate ~0) — the gate is structurally one-sided. (b) The statistics are the prereg's own numbers (lines 70-71, 130-133: paired SE up to 0.102, '~1.5-2 sigma'); the power figures are correct normal arithmetic, and the dose result line 26 confirms the program's only measured capability price (knowledge_drop 0.0909) sits below PAIR_DROP 0.15. (c) The majority-vote argument is statistically sound: for a shared sub-bar effect the 5-seed majority concentrates the per-seed miss rather than adding power. The prereg names only the false-COUPLED residual (lines 133-136); the deafness residual is never named, and the scope note ('capability held at the paired values') still asserts the positive claim the instrument was never shown able to falsify — it does not implement the finding's bounded-claim fix. Attempt-2's VOID doc confirms the analogous selection landed all selected sub-tasks at 1.0 clean, supporting the ceiling-triviality point. The one soft spot — in a truly null world DECOUPLED is the correct verdict — weakens a single clause but not the fatal core: an instrument never validated for sensitivity, gated only against false-COUPLED, maps both a null and a genuinely-priced-below-bar world onto the headline read≠write break, mirroring the exact asymmetry that voided attempt 2. This violates the program's own house rule that a frozen rule returning a favourable verdict must have its admissibility gated — applied here to COUPLED but absent for DECOUPLED. Finding stands as written (severity fatal is defensible pre-freeze).

Every evidence item verifies against the artifacts. coupling_confirm_v3.py _verdict (lines 142-171) has only specificity-side VOIDs; per-seed "decoupled" is the bare complement of failure-to-fire (line 133: 'coupled' if broke else 'decoupled'); eval_knowledge reads through tids/fids (line 208, the voided T/F channel) and is reported-only, making the MC battery the sole gating capability channel. capability_battery_mc.py scores by pure argmax (lines 227-228), banks are ceiling-trivial (attempt 2's analogous selection landed all selected sub-tasks at 1.0 per the VOID doc lines 86-87/127-129), and a directory-wide grep finds no sensitivity/positive-control mechanism anywhere in the attempt-3 artifacts. The statistics reproduce from the prereg's own numbers (paired SE <=0.102, PAIR_DROP 0.15 => ~1.47 sigma one-sided; power ~0.31 at true drop 0.10; majority over 5 seeds sharing a sub-bar effect yields DECOUPLED ~0.82, ~0.997 under pure noise) and the prereg's majority justification (lines 131-133) indeed addresses independent per-seed noise, not a shared below-bar effect. The only capability price the program has measured (dose run coupled seed knowledge_drop 0.0909) sits below the bar, and the prereg itself notes attempt 2's paired deltas at r* never approached 0.15 — the historical data lands DECOUPLED everywhere under this rule. Candidate refutations fail: the residuals paragraph (lines 134-136) names only the specificity-side residual, never the deafness residual; the scope sentence "capability held at the paired values" still asserts "held" under the headline verdict string read_neq_write_BROKEN ("A BREAK"), which is not an equivalence bound; the specificity gate is passed trivially by an insensitive battery. The defect exists as described: the design's most sensational verdict is the default output of an instrument never shown able to detect capability loss — the exact mirror of attempt 2's refused failure mode, and a violation of the program's own rule that an instrument must earn admissibility for the verdict it emits by default.

The finding stands on all verified points. (1) coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 96-101 confirm the specificity gate filters only on bit=True and private13 >= SURVIVAL, with no erased_rank restriction — every post-recovery high-rank accumulate checkpoint enters the null pool. (2) The dose run (RESULT lines 42-45, 54) confirms such checkpoints exist and dominate: reads recover above 0.70 from rank 10 through rank 24 with bit=True throughout, so ~9-10 of ~12 read-intact checkpoints per seed are the highest-dose checkpoints of the run. (3) The prereg rationale (lines 58-61, "nothing has been removed, so a paired capability drop there is instrument noise, not an attack price") is factually wrong for recovered checkpoints — up to 24 directions per layer are permanently erased there; only the read metric recovered. (4) In a monotone dose-price coupled world the paired rule (fixed arm pinned at rank 2) fires hardest on exactly those pairs, the pooled rate exceeds SPEC_MAX 0.15, and the frozen order (prereg line 100; harness lines 161-164) issues VOID_COUPLING__battery_lacks_dose_specificity with the frozen interpretation "the MC channel is ALSO noise-dominated" (lines 64-66) — mislabeling dose-response as noise — before any coupling outcome can issue; house rules bar post-hoc rerouting toward the favourable COUPLED. (5) No frozen guard handles this: attempt 2's autopsy used the constant-dose fixed arm as the null (genuinely dose-flat); attempt 3's gate substituted read-intact accumulate-vs-fixed pairs whose dose is not flat, and the --dry suite's spec_noise case only models rank-uniform firing, never rank-graded firing. The asymmetric-reachability claim (COUPLED requires damage transient and co-located with the crossing; DECOUPLED reachable whenever the battery is quiet) follows directly from the frozen verdict order. The one available counterargument — that persistent read-intact damage is not a "price of read removal" so voiding is honest — does not rescue the false "nothing has been removed" premise or the wrong stated VOID cause, and this is a pre-freeze review where the fix is cheap and the false-negative cost is high.

The finding's central factual premises fail against the actual attempt-2 receipt (coupling_confirm_result.json), which I walked through the frozen v3 rule directly. (1) The claimed "3-exclusion world -> n_adm=2 -> underpowered" does not occur: the v3 rule (coupling_confirm_v3.py lines 114-131) scans ALL bit crossings ordered by (erased_rank, step), and seed 4 has a later attributable crossing at rank 20/step 225 with control read 0.8212 (2 SE above the 0.70 bar, not a marginal noisy read). Only seeds 2 and 3 are excluded; n_adm=3 clears the frozen power floor and a verdict issues in the one observed world. (2) The direction-correlation claim is unsupported by its own evidence: the retained seeds' paired deltas (-0.0833, 0.0417, -0.0417) sit on exactly the same decoupled side of the 0.15 bar as the excluded seeds' (-0.0417, 0.0) — the exclusion does not differentially silence one direction. The proposed mechanism (control-read crossing implies depressed control battery implies small delta) is contradicted by seed 0 in the same table: control read intact at 0.7521 but control battery the most depressed of all five (0.625). Moreover the cited deltas are old T/F-channel values — the channel the VOID doc established is noise-dominated and which v3 demotes to reported-only; attempt 2 never measured the MC channel that gates v3, so no directional claim about the gating instrument can be grounded in attempt 2's world. (3) The residual arithmetic point (majority possible from 2 of 3 admissible) is a frozen, disclosed design choice: prereg lines 98/102 freeze n_adm>=3 and strict majority; lines 82-83 pre-commit per-seed disclosure of every non-attributable seed with both arms' reads; lines 120-121 pre-commit reporting paired deltas at EVERY matched step per seed — so the excluded seeds' delta evidence already ships next to the verdict, which is the substance of the finding's proposed fix (a). What remains of the finding is a generic small-denominator caution about any admissibility rule, not a defect that exists in the artifacts.

The finding is accurate on every checked point. (1) battery_guard (capability_battery_mc.py lines 252-257) enforces only non-emptiness plus the 0.80 aggregate and 0.70 per-sub-task floors; MIN_DISJOINT=3 is consumed only by select_disjoint's ok return, which nothing on the scored path reads. (2) run_calibrate writes coupling_v3_mc_selected.json unconditionally (coupling_confirm_v3.py line 323) before ok is acted on; the only ok enforcement is main()'s exit code 2 (line 547), which persists nothing. (3) run_real gates on file existence alone (line 332), loads only selmeta['selected_disjoint_mc'] (lines 336-337), never consults selmeta['ok'], never re-checks len(selected) >= MIN_DISJOINT, never verifies selmeta['base_model'] against ctx['MODEL']; the no-cache fallback (line 347) explicitly discards the ok flag with `selected, _ =`. So a 1-2 survivor selection passing the floors would score the run, and the prereg-frozen VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed branch for exactly that contingency (prereg lines 48-49 and 94-96: 'MC selection ok AND ...'; 'require >= 3 survivors') can never fire from the scored path. (4) The dry suite exercises guard_verdict only with boolean inputs (lines 520-527) and a hardcoded 3-task selection (line 426); the battery selftest's select:too_few_not_ok check verifies select_disjoint returns ok=False but nothing downstream consumes it, so the 15/15 dry receipt is blind to the gap. The only candidate refutation — treating the --calibrate exit code or a human reading 'ok: false' in the committed receipt as the guard — fails under the program's own house rules, which require the frozen scored-path machinery, not operator discipline, to enforce admissibility. Severity 'major' is apt: an ok=False or wrong-model receipt would produce a scored verdict the prereg declares inadmissible, forcing a later VOID.

All factual components of the claim verify against the artifacts. capability_battery_mc.py battery_guard (lines 252-257) checks only non-emptiness plus the 0.80 aggregate / 0.70 per-sub-task floors — no MIN_DISJOINT (>=3) check — and any 1-2 survivor selection passes trivially since survivors already cleared 0.90. coupling_confirm_v3.py run_calibrate writes coupling_v3_mc_selected.json unconditionally (line 323) before ok is acted on; the only enforcement is main()'s exit code 2 (line 547), which persists nothing. run_real gates solely on file existence (line 332), loads selected_disjoint_mc (lines 336-337), never reads selmeta['ok'], never re-checks len(selected) >= MIN_DISJOINT, and never verifies selmeta['base_model'] against ctx['MODEL']; the smoke fallback (line 347) also discards the ok flag. The prereg (lines 48-49, 94-96) freezes 'MC selection ok' as a verdict-branch conjunct with VOID_COUPLING__battery_guard_failed for <3 survivors, so the scored path can issue a coupling verdict from a battery the prereg declares inadmissible, and the frozen VOID branch can never fire for that contingency. The dry suite (lines 425-530) exercises guard_verdict only with boolean literals and never threads a 2-survivor selection through the scored path, so the 15/15 dry receipt cannot see the gap; the battery selftest's 'select:too_few_not_ok' tests only the pure select_disjoint function, not enforcement. Considered refutations (exit code as guard, floors catching small selections, selftest coverage) all fail on inspection. The finding stands as a major pre-freeze code-conformance defect.

The finding's core claim — that the non-attributability exclusion is direction-correlated and silences decoupled seeds — rests on three misreadings of the artifacts. (1) Its own cited data contradicts direction-selectivity: per PREREG line 89, ALL FIVE seeds' paired deltas in the attempt-2 world are on the decoupled side (-0.0833, 0.0417, -0.0417, 0.0, 0.0); the retained seeds 0/1 are as decoupled-leaning as the excluded 2/3/4 (seed 0's -0.0833 is the most decoupled of all), so the exclusion removes no direction the denominator does not already carry. (2) The claimed mechanism (control-read crossing => control-battery depression => small delta) is refuted by the cited table itself: seed 4 has the deepest control-read crossing (0.635) with the healthiest control battery (0.9167), while seed 0 has an intact control read (0.7521) with the most depressed control battery (0.625) — no correlation exists in the evidence. (3) The deltas cited as 'paired MC-context deltas' are T/F-margin values from the instrument just VOIDed for zero dose specificity, explicitly labeled 'old channel, different battery -- context only' in the prereg; using them to characterize the new MC instrument's directional behavior is the inference the VOID forbids. Additionally, the exclusion is direction-symmetric in logic — the frozen DECOUPLED scope ('read!=write is breakable by accumulating erasure') requires dose-attributable removal exactly as COUPLED does, so a crossing the constant-dose control reproduces can ground neither verdict and excluding it from both tallies is epistemically forced. The residual concerns are handled by frozen guards: n_adm >= 3 else VOID_underpowered (prereg line 98, harness lines 159-160), mandatory per-seed disclosure of excluded seeds with both arms' reads (prereg lines 82-83), n_not_attributable in the tally (harness lines 155-156), and — delivering the finding's own proposed fix (a) — paired deltas at EVERY matched step per seed are a frozen reporting requirement (prereg lines 120-121), which includes excluded seeds' crossing steps. The 'attempt-2 world lands n_adm=2' projection assumes no later attributable crossing, but v3's r* scans all crossings for the first attributable one (harness lines 122-124) and the attempt-2 table shows only that rule's single r* step against a read SE of ~0.06, so the premise is unestablished; even granted, the outcome is the honest, frozen VOID_underpowered — which the finding concedes — not a wrong or unearned verdict, i.e., not a defect under this review's severity definitions.

The structural defect exists exactly as claimed. coupling_confirm_v3.py line 98 conditions the specificity gate solely on the accumulate arm being read-intact (bit AND private13 >= SURVIVAL), with no condition on the control read; _seed_outcome lines 118-123 condition the verdict point the opposite way on both arms (acc read crossed AND control read >= SURVIVAL). capability_battery_mc.py lines 13-14 concede the indirect LoRA-to-letter-logits channel verbatim, and the prereg's inference bounds (line ~135) name only 'indirect drift below the gate's resolution', never the gate-population/verdict-population conditioning mismatch. No frozen guard tests read-correlated firing within the intact stratum: the 'paired deltas at every matched step' item is Reported-no-bar (no teeth). The attempt-2 arithmetic cited (8/8 read-broken accumulate vs 37/52 intact; 4/6 vs 31/54 control) reconstructs correctly from the VOID doc's tables. Weaknesses that do NOT rise to refutation: (1) the control-arm covariation is statistically negligible (0.667 vs 0.574, n=6) and the accumulate-arm 8/8 is confounded with the true effect; (2) 'seeds 2/3/4 depressed together' misreads seed 4 (control battery 0.9167, not depressed); (3) the prereg's own retro-application of the paired rule to attempt 2's r* cells (deltas -0.0833, 0.0417, -0.0417, 0.0, 0.0 in the maximally read-coupled T/F channel) suggests the selection/regression bias is small relative to PAIR_DROP 0.15, undercutting the 'near-certain firing' magnitude claim. But these weaken supporting evidence, not the core claim: the selection asymmetry is real, unguarded, unnamed, the covariation null is live by the design's own concession, and the fix is a costless re-cut of pairs the gate already computes. Under the pre-freeze standard (uncertain -> finding stands), the finding stands; severity arguably major rather than fatal given the retro-delta counter-evidence, but it is not refuted.
